NAME: GOLEM’S CROWN
SUBJECT: RUNOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A thin, brassy diadem lined with intricate runes, and a clear band running through the middle that lights up with a liquid gold glow when activated. Touch the Crown and roll Endurance, DC5, after which time it activates for 30 seconds per degree of success, flooding your body with the resolute force of a stone golem. While active, you are immune to Doubt, and you can move at twice your normal speed, and if you are moving at top speed, you become encased with a spectral golden barrier covered with runes that protect you from anything in front of you. Any physical object will not harm you, and any spell must beat your artifact check + 10 in order to get through the barrier, ending the artifact effect early if it does. The artifact becomes inert for an hour afterwards, unable to be activated again.

NAME: POCKET OF ENCHANTMENT
SUBJECT: ENCHANTMENT
DESCRIPTION: A small velvet drawstring bag with intricate designs marked in gold. You can draw out a pinch of a fine shimmering crystal dust at any time and sprinkle it on top of an enchanted object, rolling Improvise, DC5, and on success, you may place a second enchantment on the object, which will last for 10 minutes per degree of success on the artifact check. The use of this artifact counts as the 100 materials needed to place the enchantment. The two enchantments on the object may interact in unpredictable ways, but if you’re an enchanter, you’re probably used to things acting weirdly.

NAME: CREEPING SHADOW
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A pair of stylish black lace gloves with tiny runes woven into the fingertips. If you stand still in one place for 5 minutes, then at the end of that time, you can roll Concentration, DC10 and become invisible for one minute per degree of success, as long as you continue holding still. You can repeat the roll at the end of the time period to remain invisible, but if you backfire, you become briefly visible.

NAME: WISHING VIAL
SUBJECT: ALCHEMY
DESCRIPTION: A small, crystalline decanter that shimmers with a clear fluid inside. Once per day, you can roll Improvise, DC9 to speak a command word and pour it into a vial, instantly creating any potion of 50 materials for free. On 3 or more degrees of success, you instantly create any potion of 100 materials or less for free.

NAME: THORN MASTER’S CRADLE
SUBJECT: HERBALISM
DESCRIPTION: A set of twisted, interlaced weaves that look like brambles, worn on each arm, that glow with a faint green light when activated. While this artifact is equipped, your cultivation capacity increases to 100 * your Herbalism ranks, and you can sense anytime anybody is close to any of your cultivated plants, although you can’t tell who it is. You can activate this artifact and roll Willpower, DC16 or versus the Intensity Rank of a target plant, if applicable, to either take psychic control of a plant in sight or of any of your cultivated plants, for 5 minutes.

NAME: ARITANA’S SEAL
SUBJECT: RUNOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A small ring in a dark, enchanted metal with a soft shimmering green color, it lights up with ancient runic designs when you channel its power to create a rune. While this artifact is equipped, you can take an extra degree of success to every Runic Blessing you cast, and any time you create a rune, you can roll Investigate, DC15 to feel the rune’s possibilities. On success, the GM will present two possible outcomes for what the rune will do, and you can choose between them. Extra degrees of success add more options for you to choose from.

NAME: WANDMASTER’S EYE
SUBJECT: WANDCRAFTING
DESCRIPTION: An intricate, finely designed frame worn along one ear and over one eye, with a ring of solid lunar platinum in it. While this artifact is equipped, you can take a +2 to Wandcrafting, and you can roll Read Person, DC15 to glean one piece of information per degree of success about a person whose wand you can see. You can’t choose what kind of information it is you get, so sometimes you will get information that you don’t want to know.

NAME: AVIDAR’S LIQUID SKIN
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A pair of dark turquoise gloves that slip on with a skintight fit and feel cool and slightly wet to the touch. While this artifact is equipped, you can touch anything regardless of how hot it is, and you can activate the artifact by rolling Endurance, DC13. On success, the gloves expand to cover your whole body with a slick second skin that lets you breathe underwater, move at running speed in any direction underwater, and as long as you stay still, become invisible while underwater. This effect lasts indefinitely, but it ends if you ever spend 1 minute out of the water at a time.

NAME: CELESTIAL GIFT
SUBJECT: RESTORATION
DESCRIPTION: A pair of pure white bangles that always feel pleasantly cool to the touch. While this artifact is equipped, people tend to be more soothed and relaxed around you. You can also touch somebody and roll Win Over, DC14, giving them a +4 to one type of Resist check of your choice per degree of success for the next hour. When you use this ability on someone, the celestial magic effect can leave them feeling oddly enamored with you until it ends, although any PC can choose not to be affected. The magic also makes your skin softer and smoother while the bangles are equipped, so it’s popular with the influencer girlies who like to then pretend they have a great skincare routine and sell you something.

NAME: KALON’S CHAIN
SUBJECT: TELEKINESIS
DESCRIPTION: A set of small chains you can keep affixed anywhere on your person that glow vermillion when used. While this artifact is equipped, you can telekinetically draw any object towards you, as a spectral chain connects between you and the target object reeling it in. This pull is weak and cannot get past any meaningful resistance, but you can roll Concentration, DC15 to wrench objects more firmly towards you, breaking through obstacles or out of the grasp of anything that doesn’t beat your check.

NAME: BLACK ROD
SUBJECT: TRANSMUTATION
DESCRIPTION: A small, twisted rod in abyssal steel with a small goldenrod crystal at each end, glowing with a faint transmutative magic. You can touch the Rod to an object and roll Concentration, DC13 to break down the target object, searing a hole into it, bigger the more degrees of success you have. The artifact has no effect on warded or runed objects, but is effective on almost anything else, no matter the composition.

NAME: TOME OF TRUTH AND LIE
SUBJECT: ARCANE HISTORY
DESCRIPTION: A magic book that floats at your command and is written in an arcane script only the one attuned to it can read. You can roll Research, DC12 to draw from its knowledge filled with magical accounts of deceit in order to take a +2 per degree of success on a Deception check or a Read Person check to tell if someone is being honest. The tome continually creates and dismisses its own pages, making it a living thing that will sometimes play and create fun texts or images, but sometimes its images are a little too fond of girls in bunny outfits. Or guys in bunny outfits.

NAME: MARWYN’S EYE
SUBJECT: DIVINATION
DESCRIPTION: A small but heavy eye cast in ornate charcoal metal with a single ley tanzanite core set in the middle of an iris design. You can sweep the Eye over an area and detect automatically if anything you can see is totally illusionary, although you cannot tell if something is just illusorily disguised as something else, just if something is completely an illusion. You can also activate the Eye by rolling Concentration, DC12, lighting up the ley tanzanite in a brilliant indigo glow. On success, you can see any place you’ve been in the past twelve hours per degree of success, and you can watch for one minute, seeing the place but not perceiving it in any other way. Finally, you can just magically check to see if you left the stove on at home.

NAME: STORYWEAVER
SUBJECT: ARCANE HISTORY
DESCRIPTION: A fountain pen in dark burnished gold with a ley line flower design subtly etched into the length of it, it draws faintly shimmering lines in the air that disappear shortly after. While you have this artifact equipped, you have more say over the outcome of Inflection Points than you would otherwise. In addition, you can roll Deception, DC14 to write a story into the ley lines. On success, any number of people you choose who hear what you say must roll Recall Information versus your check + 6, and for each degree of failure, you can choose one thing that they fully believe has happened. After 10 minutes, their memories return, usually leaving them confused. Sometimes they just shrug it off, though. Some people have been gaslit a lot.

NAME: BECKONING BLOSSOM
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: An earring charm with a faint pink crystal that appears ordinary until further inspection reveals it as activated fey quartz. While this artifact is equipped, you are aware of anybody’s attempts to magically influence somebody’s thoughts or feelings around you, including your own. You can touch the earring and activate its magic against a person, rolling Win Over, DC15. On success, the target must roll Willpower versus your check or have their feelings about one person changed for each degree of failure on their check, for one hour, or you can spend two degrees to make their feelings persist for 24 hours. If you change someone’s feelings about somebody else too drastically with this, they are likely to realize they’ve been manipulated. A PC can always choose not to be affected or to be affected for less time.

NAME: TRAPKEEPER
SUBJECT: RUNOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A small, intricately carved rod made of arcanum scyllus wood, capped with a gold tip with a single radiant runic design. While this artifact is equipped, you know the Glyph spell if you don’t already, and if it backfires, you can choose for this artifact to backfire instead of rolling Resist. When you do cast Glyph this way, you can roll Comprehend, DC11, to tap this artifact to the runic seal and pour magic out into it. For each degree of success, you can increase Glyph’s degrees of success by 1, plant an additional spell, or name a field of magic and the GM will pick a spell from that field that you can place in the Glyph even if you don’t know it. When you do this, the Glyph placed is undetectable until it activates, which often makes it very, very detectable.

NAME: SPELL PAINTER’S PALETTE
SUBJECT: ENCHANTMENT
DESCRIPTION: A pair of rose-gold bangles inlaid with countless tiny runes on the inside, storing aspects of thousands of spells. While this artifact is active, you can take a +4 to all checks to identify a given spell that you see cast or see the results of its casting. In addition, whenever you set out to place an enchantment, you can activate this artifact by rolling Recall Information, DC10, to summon a spectral sheet of spell aspects. For each degree of success, the GM will offer access to one extra spell you don’t know to place as an enchantment.

NAME: INDIGO FLOWER
SUBJECT: ARCANE HISTORY
DESCRIPTION: A small crystalline flower in an iridescent pale indigo that grants a connection to the endless knowledge of the ley lines. When you first meet someone, or when you meet someone for the first time in a while, you can roll Recall Information, DC10, to tap into the knowledge of the ley lines and their course of the fate of all things, as the flower’s petals glow brighter. For each degree of success, you can learn one piece of information about the person, including about their fate and likely futures. You can spend a Downtime block meditating with this artifact, refreshing your ability to use it again on everybody you come across as if you’re meeting them anew.

NAME: MIREL’S WATERCOLOR
SUBJECT: ENCHANTMENT
DESCRIPTION: A simple gold ring worn around the index finger, with a delicate insignia in the center and a single band of pale color that flows around the length of it like a liquid, slowly shifting hue. When you first attune to this artifact, you can choose any spell you know or any Standard or Basic spell, and if you don’t know that spell yet, you become able to place it as an enchantment. When you set out to enchant an object with this spell, you can activate the ring by rolling Art, DC15. On success, you create a flow of delicate colors that swirl into the target object in a precise routine that stores the magic of the chosen spell, allowing you to place that enchantment without spending the 100 materials to do so. If the check fails or backfires, the enchantment still costs you the regular 100 materials. With a period of Downtime, you can choose a different spell to store in this artifact.

NAME: EXTRAPLANAR HEART
SUBJECT: SUMMONING
DESCRIPTION: A brassy disc inlaid with strange, extraplanar magic insignias and seems to subtly beat like a heart, worn by planting it into the chest, where it integrates itself with the wearer’s body. While this artifact is equipped, you are immune to extraplanar stress, and you can activate it by rolling Athletics, DC13. On success, you can draw in the power of an extraplanar entity to empower your physical form, gaining a +4 to Body and a +2 to one Skill of your choice per degree of success, for 5 minutes, during which time you cannot cast spells or use this artifact again. The artifact gains a tiny insignia for every extraplanar creature whose strength you’ve drawn from this way, creating a chronicle of your extraplanar connections, and extraplanar creatures are likely to respect you if you have a large collection of different insignias, especially from uses with high degrees of success, representing more powerful extraplanar creatures.

NAME: AILEE’S WHISPER
SUBJECT: HYPNOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A simple band worn around the hand with a conduit in the palm, which blossoms into a spectral lotus flower when the artifact is activated. You can roll Stealth, DC12 to create a spectral flower that emits a small pink wisp and sends it towards a target, who must roll Perception versus your check or be marked without noticing it. When you have someone marked, you can tell when they’re asleep and what they’re dreaming about, and you can cast Hypnomancy spells on them to influence their dreams as if you are physically with them. This mark is expended when you cast a Hypnomancy spell on them this way, or you can dismiss it at any point. You can only have one person marked in this way at a time. If the target is willing, they can choose to fail the Perception roll against your check, but you still must roll to activate the artifact. The artifact designer, Ailee, is mostly anonymous and just known for being a dreamcreep nobody likes, but we have to admit, her designs are pretty solid. Credit where credit is due.

NAME: COSMIC REPOSITORY
SUBJECT: CHRONOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: An artifact that looks tastefully like a sleek, understated gold watch, expanding into a spectral machination of countless spinning gears revealing information from different timelines. While this artifact is equipped, you have an innate, automatic sense for whether something interesting has happened recently in the area you are in, and the GM will sometimes give insights on it for free. You can roll Search, DC12 to pore through the information presented by the Repository and receive one interesting piece of information per degree of success about the area you are in, including anything interesting that has happened in it recently. The artifact has its own sense of what constitutes remarkable, interesting incidents, and is inexplicably fascinated with beetroot, and will often let you know if somebody has had a beet salad recently.

NAME: SILVER-SCALE CREST
SUBJECT: DRACONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A band of shimmering draconic scale worn anywhere on the face, where it shifts to accommodate its placement, becoming one with the wearer. While this artifact is equipped, any time you encounter materials from a Downtime option or from an unexpected boon, you can take an extra 100, as the Crest guides you to find more, driven by the draconic treasure-hoarding instinct. You can activate the artifact by rolling Investigation or Search, DC12, lighting up your irises in glowing silver as the Crest guides you towards treasure. For each degree of success, you can get a clue about the location of materials, a potion, a wand, an artifact, or a clue or a piece of relevant information to what you are after.

NAME: LUMA’S LANTERN
SUBJECT: NECROMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A heavy artifact like a small gas lantern in dull black metal and a spectral silver flame inside. While this artifact is equipped, you can cast light with it even in magical darkness. You can activate the artifact by rolling Creature or Persuasion, DC13 to open a door on the lantern and let the spectral flame out, a necromantic spirit like a will-o’-the-wisp that follows your directions. It cannot physically interact and has 0 Resolve, meaning it is dispersed if it is ever forced to Resist, but it can perceive everything around it like a person and it can pass through anywhere that light can pass through. It can remain indefinitely outside of the lantern, but you cannot tell anything it has perceived until it is closed inside the lantern again, at which point you become aware of everything it perceived. You can instantly summon it back into the lantern from anywhere when it is out.

NAME: SANGUINE GLOW
SUBJECT: ENCHANTMENT
DESCRIPTION: A pure white crystal device that connects to the raw physical essence of the user’s blood. You can turn and realign the various forms and pieces of the device to interact with the bioarcane link and pour magic force into your blood, causing the crystal to glow with a bright blood red. Roll Endurance, DC13. For each degree of success, you can choose one Skill to take a +4 for the next hour. If you fail, you must Resist, DC10. Whether it succeeds or fails, once you have used it, you cannot use it again for one hour.

NAME: MEYRI’S MAGIC CLAY
SUBJECT: WANDCRAFTING
DESCRIPTION: An earthen-red clay in the form of a serpent that, if left alone, hangs in place in the air moving in an infinity symbol, shimmering with a pale magic light. You can form the clay into a wand, spending an hour and rolling Art, DC10. On success, you can obtain any Standard or Basic wand of your choice without spending the materials for it, or on 4 or more degrees of success, you can obtain an Advanced wand this way. You must equip the new wand right away, and once you unequip the wand, it collapses back into its original form and can be formed into a new wand.

NAME: ARCLIGHT’S FLASH STEP
SUBJECT: COUNTERHEXOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A heavy pendant inscribed with neat, interlocking lines that glow with a faint protective magic. While this artifact is equipped, you have advanced warning when someone is about to cast a Standard or higher spell or artifact effect on you, as well as whether that spell or artifact is hexological. When you are targeted by a spell or artifact effect and you can roll a Body Skill to avoid being affected, you can roll Agility with this artifact instead, against the same DC, as the pendant flashes with a brilliant light and helps move you out of the way. An FDM classic, popular among people meddling in the affairs of dangerous rogue arcanists.

NAME: FIRE-EYES
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A magic charm that looks more like a cute accessory than an artifact of elemental force, in the shape of a cute animal head whose eyes light up with spectral fire when you activate the artifact. Roll Creature, DC13. On success, you can summon a small elemental creature of whatever element you choose, a creature of your choice no bigger than your head made of pure elemental energy, with 1 Resolve, which rolls 0/5. The creature lasts for 10 minutes, during which time it obeys your spoken instructions to the best of its ability. It’s also pretty cute, honestly. Keep in mind you still can’t hug fire elementals, no matter how cute they are.

NAME: NEPHERA’S WICKED CLOUD
SUBJECT: HERBALISM
DESCRIPTION: A brace of tangled brambles worn over the hand, which causes them to sink into your hand and become part of you, looking like a tattoo of thorned vines all over one hand. You can grip your hand and channel the magic through the vines to become one with the herbalist magics, rolling Agility, DC15. On success, you are instantly transformed into a cloud of deep violet spores for 5 seconds, during which time you can move at high speed in any direction and slip through any opening that water can go through. When you coalesce again, you come back together feeling like your bones have been disassembled and put back in place—kind of like a chiropractor, just less scary.

NAME: DANCING DREAM
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A small, silvery tiara that gleams with fey quartz and a dusting of pixie dust, it appears to project a halo around your head, but only when someone views you from the corner of their eye, disappearing when they look at you directly. While this artifact is equipped, you can project your sight to any point you can see, and gain a glimpse of sight as if you were looking from that point. You can also activate the artifact by speaking a name chosen when you first attune to this artifact, which calls forth the artifact’s spirit as an illusory figure. Roll Perception, DC11. On success, you summon an illusory figure that looks like whatever you want but is easily detectable as an illusion (Perception or Analyze DC5), but can move freely in three dimensions and through anything light can pass through, and you can freely alternate between perceiving through your senses or that of the illusory figure. The illusory figure follows your directions, at least by the letter, but can be a little playful and… creative with the interpretations. Once summoned, it lasts for 10 minutes.

NAME: TRUNDALE’S TRUNK
SUBJECT: TELEPORTATION
DESCRIPTION: A small container made of banded wood with an ornate lock on it that bears the great mage trickster Trundale’s seal on it. While this artifact is equipped, you can place anything inside the Trunk to make it impossible for anyone else to access, and no magic can detect the existence of anything inside the Trunk. You can also roll Sleight of Hand, DC14, to open a portal between the Trunk and a target, either moving the contents of the Trunk to the target location or stealing a target object into the Trunk. If you are attempting to steal from or plant an object on a person with this, they can roll Perception versus your check to catch it and stop it, but unless they have 3 or more degrees of success, they won’t be able to track exactly what’s happening back to you. Unless you’re standing in front of them giggling while you reach your hand into the trunk. That might clue them in.

NAME: AELYN’S SHIELD
SUBJECT: WARDING
DESCRIPTION: A delicate, ornate choker with a band of lunar silver along the middle, shimmering with a faint protective energy. While this artifact is active, any time a spell check forces you to make a check and you fail with one degree of failure, it is instead treated as one degree of failure and one degree of success. In addition, you can activate it by rolling Analyze, DC13 and choosing one field of magic or one specific spell to protect against per degree of success. For each field of magic chosen, you take a +4 to all checks made to resist effects from that field of magic for the next hour, and for each specific spell chosen, you become immune to that spell for the next hour. At the GM’s discretion, you may also choose nonmagical situations of equal specificity to protect against, such as taking a +4 to protect from harm from falling from heights or becoming immune to a certain type of weapon. Whether the effect succeeds or fails, you cannot attempt to activate it again for an hour.

NAME: SERPENT OF DREAD
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A small sculpture of the Abyssal Serpent who writhes in the deepest roots of the demonic plane, carved from deep-rock abyssal stone. While this artifact is equipped, you have a +4 to all Charm checks to make someone feel uneasy, and you can activate it by rolling Willpower, DC11. On success, one target you can see per degree of success must roll Willpower versus your check or feel a deep sense of unease that they’re doing something wrong, often dissuading them from what they’re doing. You can spend three degrees of success to force a target to Resist Doubt, DC14 at the same time. For such a creepy, evil-looking little thing, it’s actually primarily useful for getting people to stop making stupid decisions.

NAME: MARWYN’S NIGHT
SUBJECT: ARTIFICY
DESCRIPTION: A small, solid scepter made of whisperwood and set with the fang of a black worm, a pale blue light runs through the intricate lines carved all along the length of it, dancing from one end to the next. You can activate it by rolling Sleight of Hand or Stealth, DC12, to cloak it in shadow, dampening your magic signature. For each degree of success, you can take a +4 to one check to perform magic or use an artifact stealthily over the next hour, during which time it continues shrouded in darkness and cannot be activated again until the effect ends.

NAME: SEEKER’S SECRET
SUBJECT: HERBALISM
DESCRIPTION: A long spiral ring made from the heart of the gold-tipped seeker, a rare and powerful version of the white-tipped seeker famous in herbalist circles for tracking down anything. While this artifact is active, you always know the way back to your home as long as you are in the wilderness, and you can activate the ring, causing it to glow golden as spectral lines feel out from its bands into the air around you. Roll Tracking, DC8. For each degree of success, you obtain one clue about where a given person is, as long as they are outside or have been in the last hour. At the GM’s discretion, if the target isn’t trying to hide their location, your “clue” may simply be the target’s exact location or the exact time and location they were last outside.

NAME: HEARTSONG STONE
SUBJECT: CRYSTALLOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A magically empowered crystal, one half jet-black and one half soft pink, suspended inside a shimmering glass vial, where it turns slowly, humming with magic energy. While this artifact is equipped, you can automatically tell when someone is hiding something or not being fully upfront, but it doesn’t tell you what it is they’re hiding. You can activate the artifact by rolling Read Person, DC13, forcing a target to roll Concentration or Deception versus your check. For each degree of failure on their check, you can learn one fear, insecurity, hope, or dream that they have. You can choose for the effect to be subtle and for them not to know you gleaned this information unless they succeed on their check.

NAME: OBLIVION GOBLET
SUBJECT: TRANSMUTATION
DESCRIPTION: A heavy, elegant goblet that is filled to the top with a clear, bubbling liquid, but that never spills, even if inverted or if something is added that should make it overflow. When this artifact is equipped, you can put any small nonmagical inorganic object into the liquid, which will quickly dissolve it into nothing. You can also roll Analyze, DC14 to put any inorganic object with a material value into the goblet, which will break it down into a number of materials equal to half its value. At the GM’s discretion, any object may have too much magic power for the goblet to dissolve. You could put your friend’s wedding ring in here, if you’re a really bad friend.

NAME: CLATHRA FANG
SUBJECT: BESTIOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: An amulet made of the fang of a clathra elite, a nimble and fearsome beast, encased in phoenix fire resin and worn on a leather string. While this artifact is equipped, you can perform movement stealthily in Action without extra AP cost, and you can activate a spectral green flame from the fang by rolling Agility, DC14. On success, you tap into the elite hunter’s skill of the clathra, and for one minute, you can move with increased grace and speed, shrouding you with a faint spectral green flame and giving a +2 per degree of success to Agility and Stealth checks as well as Athletics checks to move quickly. While this effect is active, you can climb walls as easily as walking the same distance and you can jump three times higher and farther without making a check. You also crave scritches behind the ear.

NAME: FLIGHT OF FANCY
SUBJECT: ALCHEMY
DESCRIPTION: A tiny, elegant crystal vial with a crescent-moon stopper made of lunar silver, a dreamy mist spills out when you open it. You can take out the dropper in the lid and place a single droplet in your mouth, causing a swirl of magic to percolate through your body. Roll Deception, DC10. On success, you can illusorily alter one thing about your appearance for 1 hour, or on 4 degrees of success, you can alter your appearance entirely to look like another person. Anyone attempting to see through the illusion must roll Perception or Analyze versus your check + 4.

NAME: MISTWALKER’S MELODY
SUBJECT: TELEPORTATION
DESCRIPTION: A delicate fey quartz charm worn over the outside of the ear, making a soft melodic sound at the touch. While this artifact is equipped, you travel double the distance from Blink and Meyri’s Mist Walk. You can activate the artifact with precise touches to call up a music-box-like melody, rolling Art, DC13. On success, your next teleport distance is multiplied by 10 times your degrees of success on this check. Not recommended for teleporting straight up.

NAME: WHISPERKEEP WATCH
SUBJECT: DIVINATION
DESCRIPTION: An ornate headpiece like a delicate, romantic tiara or diadem, it summons a long flowing spectral veil when activated. Name a person you’ve seen before and roll Perception, DC13. On success, you can learn one piece of information per degree of success about where the target is right now or what they’re doing. This effect automatically fails if the target has Scry Shield or any other protection against divination. If you find out things you don’t want to know about a person doing this, well, that’s kind of on you.

NAME: PROTECTOR STONE
SUBJECT: COUNTERHEXOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A row of small, ornate crystals that are affixed to any point on the body the wearer chooses, where they settle in like a part of the body. While this artifact is equipped, you are immune to any hexological spell cast directly against you, and you can choose one other person to also receive this protection. You can change who is protected this way at any point without a check by touching them and speaking a command word. You can also activate the Stone by rolling Comprehend, DC14, making them flare up in different colors briefly as they search the magic presence around you. For each degree of success, you learn one thing about hexological magic used nearby recently or any magic cast secretly. You must spend 10 minutes if you ever want to remove the Stone from your person, because they have separation anxiety.

NAME: SCORPION SANDS
SUBJECT: CHRONOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A small pendant with an hourglass in the middle, with shimmering, opalescent sand that flows slowly from top to bottom. You can turn the hourglass to adjust the very recent past: roll Willpower, DC12, and for each degree of success, you can make one skill check, including using an artifact, or cast a Basic or Standard spell at any point in the past up to 5 seconds before. You can spend 3 degrees of success to cast an Advanced or higher spell. Once used in this way, the pendant takes an hour to fill up the sand in the bottom again, and it cannot be used again until all the sand has moved to the bottom.

NAME: SAVAGE GRASP
SUBJECT: WARDING
DESCRIPTION: A solid bar made of a jet-black material like obsidian that’s warm to the touch, set with dragon’s onyx in an eye-shaped design in the center. You can squeeze it tightly in one hand, calling up a flash of crackling white magic in the signature warding lattice as it converts your magic force to raw physical power. Roll Athletics, DC10. For the next ten minutes, you can take a +4 bonus to all Body checks, plus 1 for each degree of success past the first, but during this time, you cannot cast any spells or use any artifacts. You can end the effect early by gripping the artifact tightly again, pulling the magic back out of you and forcing you to Resist, DC13.

NAME: ANGEL’S WHISPER
SUBJECT: HYPNOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A silver ring inlaid with pink fey tourmaline and a delicate inscription on the inside that constantly rewrites itself into ancient celestial prose. Roll Persuasion, DC14. On a success, the ring glows with a pale pink light that only you can see, forming a tether to a person you can see before the light vanishes. For each degree of success, you can pick one statement to implant into the target’s mind, and the next time they sleep, they must roll Comprehend versus your check or be persuaded of those statements you implanted as if you’d made a Persuasion check to them directly, but they will believe any changes to their beliefs come from within. You could use it for evil, but it’s also great for giving your self-conscious friends more confidence!

NAME: SOUL STRING
SUBJECT: ARTIFICY
DESCRIPTION: A thin, crystalline band worn over the tip of your dominant index finger, where the surface swims with small ribbons of magic light. You can touch it to an object and pull a thin, spectral thread out of the object to track it back to anyone it’s connected to. Roll Art or Tracking, DC11. For each degree of success, you can gain one clue about someone who owns the object or someone who’s recently held or used it. On three or more degrees of success, you can choose to leave no trace that you interacted with the object.

NAME: HIDDEN HEART
SUBJECT: TELEKINESIS
DESCRIPTION: A perfectly round artifact that looks like ordinary metal until the person it’s attuned to picks it up, at which point it turns liquid and flows like quicksilver to coat their hand. When you have it activated in this way, you can Hide Object, DC12, to telekinetically reach out and place a small glimmer of magic on an object up to 5 kilograms per degree of success and make it invisible to everyone but yourself for 1 minute. While it is invisible in this way, you can add a bonus equal to your degrees of success to any Telekinesis spell cast on the target object. You can only have one object hidden with the Heart at a time.

NAME: TIMESPINNER
SUBJECT: CHRONOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A small charm like a roulette wheel worn on the back of the hand, glimmering with a pale golden chronomantic light. You can spin the wheel to readjust the timeline you’re in, rolling Improvise, DC10. For each degree of success, the GM will change one small detail of the current scene, as you move to a slightly different timeline. The changes will generally be in your favor, but will often be unpredictable. You can spend 3 degrees of success to instead choose one specific small change you want, at the GM’s discretion. Originally called Wheel of Fortune, it predated the game show but still lost the copyright battle. The real rough blow was that in the aftermath, they changed the name to Wheel of Time, which didn’t last either.

NAME: ABYLAR’S GUARD
SUBJECT: BESTIOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A coarse piece of trollbone craftwork that can be fixed to a necklace or hair ornament, where a spectral light glows through the intricate design carved into it. While this artifact is equipped, you can immediately sense if the space you are in is marked as a beast’s territory, although you cannot tell what type of beast. When you are not in a beast’s territory, you can claim the current area as your own, rolling Tracking, DC15. On success, you summon a spectral creature that takes whatever form you like and patrols the area. Any creature that challenges it for territory must roll Willpower against your check, dispelling it on a success. Any time you enter your territory, you become instantly aware of everything that’s happened in the territory since the last time you were in it. The great wizard Abylar created this artifact to have terrible, powerful guards, but for some reason he could never get it to make anything other than chinchillas. They were cute, though.

NAME: DRAGON’S BEATING HEART
SUBJECT: DRACONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A gemstone earring subtly in the shape of a dragon’s red eye. While this artifact is equipped, any time you cast an Advanced or lower Evocation spell, you can choose to Resist, DC12 to add 1 degree of success to the roll. You can also activate the gemstone by rolling Stealth, DC15. On success, your magic is routed to the gemstone to flow from inside you instead of from the natural world, and for 10 minutes, your magic cannot be tracked or detected by any other magic, and you can still cast inside most types of antimagic, at the GM’s discretion. The downside is that it makes you want to snack on gemstones a little bit.

NAME: HERITAGE CHAIN
SUBJECT: NECROMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A pale, weathered chain where every link is a slightly different tone and shimmers with a slightly different magic to the touch. While you are interacting with someone, you can roll Research, DC12 to activate the Chain’s magic and psychically inspect the target’s bloodline. For each degree of success, you can gain one piece of information about them that a relative has known, or learn the name and burial location of one deceased relative. If you communicate with a deceased relative you’ve learned about this way, that relative will be more likely to be friendly to you.

NAME: SLIPSTONE
SUBJECT: SUMMONING
DESCRIPTION: A perfectly smooth stone that refracts odd colors brighter than the light around it, harvested from planes that gloomcatchers occupy, humanoid creatures known to be able to slip into any space. While this artifact is equipped, you can see clearly even in total darkness, including magical darkness, and any time you encounter a material light can pass through but you cannot, such as glass or a grate, you can roll Stealth, DC13. If the surface is not magically warded, then on success, you pass through, and for each degree of success past the first, you can bring an additional person with you. If the surface is magically warded, you do not pass through, but you learn one relevant thing about the ward per degree of success.

NAME: BLESSING OF MIDNIGHT
SUBJECT: RESTORATION
DESCRIPTION: An artifact that’s difficult to wear mostly from a stylistic perspective, a dramatic choker collar made of an intricate weave of lunar silver and black lunar platinum. You can tap the front of the collar and draw out a small orb of pure white light, rolling Hide Object, DC11 and taking one orb per degree of success. You can give someone, including yourself, one of these orbs to bestow a celestial blessing to make them harder to detect for 10 minutes, during which time any check to find them that does not beat your Hide Object check will not find them, even if it beats any other check they make to avoid detection.

NAME: ABYSSAL WORM
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A twisted bangle in the shape of a snake that slowly turns around your wrist when worn. While this artifact is equipped, you can sense automatically when something powerful or relevant is hidden in the space you are in. You can activate the Worm by rolling Tracking, DC13. On success, the bangle animates as a long, thin worm of pure darkness, slipping off your wrist and moving freely. The Worm can move into any space that water can fit into, and it behaves as a creature with 2 Resolve and rolling 0/8. For each degree of success, you can give it one task that it will attempt to accomplish to the best of its ability and then return to you, or you can instantly summon it back to your wrist at any point. Either way, when it returns, it will carry anything it picked up, and you can choose to instantly be aware of everything it experienced as if seeing it through the Worm’s senses.

NAME: LURKER’S LENS
SUBJECT: CHRONOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A brassy old frame with a thick glass lens that shimmers in odd colors at different angles, like a magnifying glass. When light passes through, it falls on the room as it would have in the past, and the ornate brass dials in the frame can be tuned to fine-tune its distance into the past. You can make an Investigation or Comprehend check with the Lens, DC14, and for each degree of success, the GM will give you one piece of information on the area from a time period in the recent past of your choice. Once used, you cannot use the Lens on the same place again, unless you’re inspecting a different time period.

NAME: FABELYN’S PEN OF WHISPERS
SUBJECT: CHRONOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A brassy-gold quill pen that disappears until it is ready to be manifested in your hand, created by the great chronomancer Fabelyn while she was in her pen era. At any point, you can write a symbol unique to you onto a surface that can hold ink with this pen. Once you have, you can write in the air with the Pen, and the message will transcribe onto the surface. You can also write through time: roll Concentration, DC10. For each degree of success, you can write one word, up to five minutes in the past or the future. Writing into the past can result in unexpected temporal changes.

NAME: VAMPIRE’S FOOTSTEPS
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: Two tiny black bands that can be affixed to your ankles, upon which they sink into your skin and coat your feet and ankles in a faint black second skin. While activated in this way, you can see clearly in the dark, and you never make noise from movement unless you choose to. You can also pull up the artifact’s magic to project a terrifying visage. Roll Willpower, DC18. On success, anyone who sees you and doesn’t know it’s coming must Resist Fear, DC14. You can remove the bands from your skin at any time, but they can’t be reinserted for another 24 hours. Popular with goths.

NAME: HAND OF THE VOID
SUBJECT: WARDING
DESCRIPTION: A thin, silvery glove that appears unremarkable until it is worn on the left hand, when it turns into a silver gauntlet with rivulets of black antimagic running through the palm and fingers like blood vessels. At any point, you can place the Hand on a rune, enchanted object, wand, potion, or block a spell with it, and roll Endurance or Concentration, DC20, and block the magic effect for 5 seconds per degree of success, after which point the magic resurges like a backed-up dam. The GM may declare that a given magic effect is too strong to be blocked this way, but only truly rare magic can escape the effect. And all the rest can talk to the hand. (Artificers aren’t very funny.)

NAME: FACE STEALER
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A hollow disc that just fits into your hand on a thick golden chain, with inscriptions around the edge of it. You can hold the disc up to a person’s face and call up a flash of magic from it, and make a DC15 Deception check. On a success, the disc becomes filled with a silvery, shimmery film that moves like a trapped wall of water, and at any point, you can touch the film and instantly disguise yourself magically as the person whose face you copied for ten minutes. Anyone attempting to see through it must make a Perception check versus your Deception check +6. Once you have used the stored disguise, it disappears from the disc, and it can then be used to capture a face again. Anyone who is conscious when it flashes in their face will be able to feel it is casting magic on their face, but if they want to determine what kind, they must roll Comprehend versus your Deception check. If someone is sleeping when it is used on them, the flash will wake them up if you fail your Deception check.

NAME: DARK LADY’S WHISPER
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A small charm in the shape of a heart with a teardrop from the bottom that hangs on a strange, silky string that bears the name of whoever is attuned to it. If you are attuned to it, you can press the charm into your face, at which point it sinks into the skin, appearing like a tattoo in the shape of the charm and has an aura that makes it obvious at a glance that it is a demonic artifact. When it is in your skin, you can establish a telepathic communication with someone you can see by rolling Persuasion versus their Willpower. On success, you can communicate telepathically both ways for as long as you want to keep the channel open, and any Charm checks you make through this channel are made more irresistible by the Lady of Darkness’s insidious magic, adding one degree of success to all checks, potentially turning a failure into a success. On a failure, they know you tried to invade their mind with demonic magic, and are unlikely to appreciate it. You can remove the charm from your skin at any point, but you must Resist (DC14) when you do.

NAME: ESSENCE OF THE CRYSTAL
SUBJECT: TRANSMUTATION
DESCRIPTION: A small, crystal vial with a beautiful, ornate stopper on top. You can open it and pour out a thick, clear fluid onto an inorganic object and roll Improvise, DC12. On success, the object is changed to a material of your choice for 24 hours, during which time the vial cannot be opened again, as it slowly starts to build up more fluid inside. Only the part of the object covered by the fluid will change, which is often not enough to cover a whole object but just part of it. Smells like daisies, but you shouldn’t sniff it too close or you’ll get it on your face and turn into wood.

NAME: ELERY’S PUPPET
SUBJECT: TELEKINESIS
DESCRIPTION: An enchanted doll that’s only a little creepy. The doll looks like a human or creature of your choice, and you can mentally command it at any time to change its features to look however you want, but it will always remain six inches tall until you activate its command. Roll Concentration or Sleight of Hand, DC11, to perform with the Puppet, at which point it expands to a full-size version of the creature of your choice, up to 10 feet tall, for 1 minute per degree of success. During this time, you control it telekinetically as if it is an extension of yourself, and it can speak with whatever voice you want it to use. It cannot cast any magic, but it can perform any other checks using your stats. If it would ever have to Resist, or once its activated time ends, it collapses back into its puppet form, and you can choose whether it falls to the ground where it was or if it magically flies back to your hand. While it is active, anybody who wants to determine if it is real can make a Read Person check against your artifact check if the puppet is taking a human form, or a Creature check against your artifact check otherwise. On one degree of success, they can tell the Puppet isn’t a real person or creature, but they can’t tell anything else. On two, they can tell it’s a puppet, and on three, they can follow its magic back to you.

NAME: LI HUAN’S DIVINE WINGS
SUBJECT: RESTORATION
DESCRIPTION: A pair of thin silver bangles that shimmer softly, making you come across, somehow, as a little more trustworthy and likable. You can activate them at any point by making a DC14 Win Over check, and on success, they light up in pale blue, creating a spectral pair of shimmering white wings on your back. This state lasts for five minutes, during which time you can touch somebody and remove one Condition stack from them, and the magic flowing through them makes them immediately more likely to like you, as if succeeding on a Win Over check against them. Doing this instantly ends the state and stores the Condition stack into the bangles, which become dull and gray and lose their abilities. At any point, you can pull the Condition out and take it onto yourself, at which point the bangles regain their luster and can be used as before. You cannot pull a Condition out of yourself this way. I love your self-esteem, but you can’t charm yourself. On more degrees of success, you become more likable while the effect is active, and people like you better when you remove a Condition from them.

NAME: SACRED LETTER
SUBJECT: RUNOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A silvery baton covered in runes everywhere except for one spot right along the center. While you have this artifact attuned to you and on your person, you can take a +4 to Runology checks to read runes. You can also use it with a DC16 Research check to touch the tip of it to a rune, and on success, it will copy the rune onto the blank space on the artifact (at the GM’s discretion, a given rune may be too big or complex to fit). Once a rune is copied this way, it can be placed somewhere else by touching the baton to another surface, at which point the rune leaves the baton and is placed on the surface, reacting as if freshly created on the surface. This can often have unexpected consequences, so wear PPE.

NAME: VALEY’S LITTLE WORKSHOP
SUBJECT: ENCHANTMENT
DESCRIPTION: A miniature version of a quaint little workshop that looks like a dollhouse and contains a clockwork figure that mills around checking its tools and workbenches while it’s idle. If you have a damaged object, including an artifact or enchanted object, you can roll Analyze or Art, DC10, and on success, you can place it inside the Workshop, which magically expands to fit the object up to a maximum of five feet tall, and the clockwork figure immediately sets to work fixing it. Technically, you can still carry the Workshop around during this period, but it might be impractical if it’s too big. You can choose instead to hide it inside a magic bubble that prevents it from being found by anyone except by you coming back to the place where you hid it, but it will reappear wherever it was left once it finishes repairing. If repairing a regular object, it takes up to a day depending on the damage, at the GM’s discretion, but if repairing an enchanted object or artifact, it takes 1 day for every 250 materials that would have gone into creating the enchantment or artifact (rounding up). Once it’s finished, the clockwork figure looks really proud of itself.

NAME: ENDRYA’S HOLLOW MASK
SUBJECT: NECROMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A pure-white mask with jet-black shapes where there should be eye holes and a mouth. At any point while you are attuned to it, you can put on the Mask, and make a DC17 Concentration check, leaving your body behind on a success and ascending as an incorporeal entity that cannot be detected except by a DC40 Perception check. You can move about as quickly as you can while walking and pass through nonmagical surfaces, and you have one minute in this state per degree of success. While in this state, you can touch somebody, planting terrible emotional anguish. They must roll Comprehend versus your Concentration check or be forced to Resist. Doing this cuts the remaining time you have in this state by one minute, but you can do it if you have less than one minute left in this state, ending the effect immediately after. Once the effect ends, you snap back to your body, which stays in place unconscious and impossible to wake during the effect. Not exactly the most charming artifact to have, but I guess it’s cool if you’re a sick freak.

NAME: VASHNAR’S CROWN
SUBJECT: HERBALISM
DESCRIPTION: A woven crown made of magic vines covered in small blossoms that open when its magic is activated. You can activate its magic at any point and make a DC15 Improvise check, and on success, you create a Spore Figure, a cloud of spores that forms a copy of yourself, disguising itself with illusion magic. The Spore Figure lasts for 10 minutes, during which time it acts as a creature separate from you that you can send telepathic communications to and sense what it is sensing, from anywhere. It has 3 Resolve and rolls 4/(4 * your degrees of success), and though it is separate from you, it will always be friendly to you and always want to do what you ask. Anyone trying to see through it can roll Perception versus your Improvise check, seeing through its illusion on a success. For each degree of success past the first, you can double the time the Spore Figure is summoned or create a second Spore Figure.

NAME: STARLIT DREAMS
SUBJECT: HYPNOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A small heart-shaped bottle with a crystalline stopper that can pull out one single brilliant pink droplet from the bottle. When you do, roll Willpower, DC15, and on success, the droplet is stable. You can place the droplet into a drink or piece of food, and whoever consumes it will have to roll Willpower versus your check or fall asleep for half an hour per degree of failure. The target can choose to fail the check. If you put the droplet directly into the target’s mouth, they roll Willpower with a -8 penalty, and you can also put it on an object, in which case whoever touches that object will have to roll instead but take a +4 bonus. If you have a dreamscape of your own creation, you can choose to have someone who falls asleep this way enter that dreamscape immediately. If you fail the Willpower check to use the bottle, the droplet pulled out is unstable, bursting into a pink cloud that puts you to sleep for one minute per degree of failure. But if you’re having a slumber party, is that really even a risk?

NAME: BEAST BANISHER
SUBJECT: BESTIOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A horn made of bone, banded with thick strips of glowing golden magic. You can blow into it (1AP) to let out a low, baleful note that is particularly potent to beasts. Roll Creature, DC16, and on success, it releases a blast of magic sound. Any beasts nearby must make an Endurance roll versus your check or Resist, and if they fail the Resist check, then in addition to taking a condition, they are bound by the strips of glowing golden magic as well. A creature that has resisted the effect cannot be affected by it again until some time has passed. When you have the Beast Banisher on you, most beasts will fear you and give you clearance, but some will take it as a provocation and attack you. It’s also pretty ugly and probably won’t get you any dates.

NAME: CHRONOCLOAK
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A stopwatch that has a shutter that can block its face, replacing the display with the abstract design of a laughing face that is the signature of the artifact’s designer. While this artifact is attuned to you and on your person, you cannot be seen in chronomantic investigations, either from the past or the future, although it will only make you yourself undetectable—the way you affect the world can still be seen. You can shutter the stopwatch at any point by rolling Stealth or Sleight of Hand, DC10, and for thirty seconds per degree of success, you will become undetectable in every way. However, after the effect runs out, the shutter snaps open, and an illusory copy of yourself moves through performing everything you did while you were undetectable, making every sound you would have made during that time.

NAME: TRUTH MERCHANT
SUBJECT: DIVINATION
DESCRIPTION: A hood that can be pulled down over the eyes, where it covers them with glowing golden eye designs. While you have this artifact equipped, you can pull the hood up and activate the magic to exchange with an extraplanar divinatory entity for information. You can ask the GM any question, and the GM will name a material cost you can pay for an answer to that question, or tell you that such an answer is impossible to give, typically 50-200 materials but up to any number for massive questions that fate itself may hinge on. If you choose to pay the price, roll Persuasion, DC15. On success, you can pay the materials and obtain the GM’s answer. Further degrees of success past the first haggle the price down, reducing the cost by 50 materials for each extra degree of success (minimum 0). In addition, while this artifact is equipped, the GM may tell you when a situation or an NPC you face conceals a secret you could buy.

NAME: SCALE OF THE ABYSSAL COURT
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: An intricate banded bar just big enough to hold in your hand, with an intricate abyssal steel design on either side, perfectly balanced but that weighs down to one side with the hurt on the wielder’s heart, becoming more unbalanced the more conditions you have. When you have at least 1 condition, you can channel your thoughts through the scale and roll Comprehend, DC13. On success, you commune with a demon who offers a deal to clear a number of conditions equal to your degrees of success, in exchange for a favor the demon names. There are no consequences for refusing the deal, but you will have to roll again if you want to seek another deal another time. If you do what the demon asks, you instantly have the agreed-upon number of conditions restored, although if you restore more than 1 stack of Doubt this way, you must roll Willpower, DC15 or become possessed by a demon for 15 minutes via a loophole in the agreement.

NAME: JAR OF SOULS
SUBJECT: NECROMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A glassy container that can take different shapes, but always churns with a faintly visible swirl of magic. While you have this artifact equipped, you can detect from a distance any place where lingering soul energy remains, typically either where a creature has died recently or a place haunted by a dead spirit. When confronting lingering soul energy, you can roll Persuasion, DC16 or versus the Willpower roll of the target if it’s a creature that resists, to soothe the soul energy and place it into the jar. The jar can hold up to four distinct souls indefinitely, and at any point, you can use one without a check to do any of the following: • Place the soul into a target container, animating it with the soul for 1 hour • Take a +2 to your next spell check • Gain an insight about the current situation • Force a target to Resist Fear • If you use the container to make sourdough starter, the resulting bread pleads for its own destruction. We don’t recommend it.

NAME: SAPHI’S SANCTUARY
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A small crescent-moon pendant made of lunar silver that emanates a soft, gentle glow, making it harder for people to be angry at you even if they have reason to while you have it equipped. You can touch the bottom tip of the crescent and speak a command word that you choose when you attune to this artifact to activate its magic and roll Win Over, DC14. On success, you create a shimmering sphere of silvery magic light that covers a space one meter around you in every direction and lasts for 5 minutes, laced with subtle mind-altering demonic energies. Anyone in this sphere who you don’t specify as exempt becomes soothed, and cannot be angry unless they make a Concentration check versus your spell check + 4. For each degree of success past the first, you can double the size or the duration of the sphere.

NAME: SWEET DREAM BASTION
SUBJECT: HYPNOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A silvery diadem with delicate pink crystal designs woven in at the edges, shimmering with faint hypnomantic magics. While you have this artifact equipped, you can never be forced to enter somebody else’s dream, and your dreams cannot be altered with hypnomancy. When you restore a condition from sleep while this artifact is equipped, you can clear two stacks of the same condition. Any time you are in a dream with this artifact equipped and you are forced to Resist, you can instead use this artifact and roll Willpower, DC15 to leave the dream instantly instead of rolling Resist. You can also roll Willpower, DC15 whenever you are in a dream to use this artifact to reach out and pull one person into your dream, although if they are unwilling, they can roll Concentration versus your check to resist the effect.

NAME: CALVAR’S MERCY
SUBJECT: RESTORATION
DESCRIPTION: A band worn around the top of one arm that glows a faint pale blue when equipped. While this artifact is equipped, you can tell by looking at someone how many conditions they currently have. Any time somebody you can see is forced to Resist a non-Wound condition, you can use this artifact and roll Read Person, DC14 to create a swell of magic light down your arm as it reaches out and bolsters the mind of the target. On success, the target can take a +2 bonus for each degree of success on your check. If they still fail, you can choose to absorb the condition into the artifact instead, delaying the onset of the condition by up to an hour but preventing you from using this artifact until the target takes the condition. You can choose to release the stored condition early. A popular artifact with people-pleasers.

NAME: EYES OF THE RED DEER
SUBJECT: DIVINATION
DESCRIPTION: A pair of small ornate crystal pieces worn above either ear, the divinatory magic in them scans the surroundings and whispers reads of the situation into your mind. While this artifact is equipped, you know automatically when somebody is lying as in the spell Greater Insight, and you can take a +4 to rolls for Action. You can name a Target you want to find, including a person or an intangible like an answer to a given question, and roll Comprehend, DC14 to activate the artifact’s deeper powers. On success, you enter the Tracker state, giving a +4 to any rolls to help you find your Target. You stay in this state until you’ve found your Target or until you give up, but giving up forces you to Resist Doubt, DC13. You cannot use this ability again until you’ve left the Tracker state. Popular with Enforcers, this artifact is patented by the infamous Red Deer Guard enforcers from Schwarzwald Akademie in Germany, who are known for being serious, diligent, and extremely kinky. It’s always the repressed ones.

NAME: HAND OF THE RICH KING
SUBJECT: TRANSMUTATION
DESCRIPTION: A royal-purple artifact worn over the back of one hand, with long, thin lines running from the wrist out to the fingertips that pulsate with magic. When this artifact is equipped, every morning, you may roll Search, DC5 and take 50 materials per degree of success, as the Hand coalesces ambient matter into magic materials. This check does not trigger artifact backfires. You may also use the hand to burn materials to empower magic. When you cast a spell, you can also roll Improvise, DC10 and, for each degree of success, you can spend 150 materials to add 1 degree of success to your spell roll, possibly changing a failure to a success.

NAME: HUNTER’S STAFF
SUBJECT: DRACONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A heavy oaken staff engraved with draconic symbols down the length of it, a dragon’s head carved at the top. You can drive the tip of the staff into the ground and roll Willpower, DC18, turning the current space into a Sanctuary, claiming it as your own like a dragon claiming their lair. Choose one option for your Sanctuary: • Hidden: anyone trying to find it must roll Perception, DC20 • Warded: anyone trying to enter must roll Willpower, DC20 • Barricaded: anyone trying to enter must roll Athletics, DC20 • You can always choose to let anyone through without a check. When you leave your Sanctuary, you can choose one person you know at least one thing about and roll Willpower, DC18. For each degree of success, you can take a +4 to checks to find them and track them down.

NAME: NASCENT SPELLWEAVER
SUBJECT: ARCANE HISTORY
DESCRIPTION: A small locket that cannot be opened, embossed with impossibly small, intricate designs said to tell a story from the dawn of magic, and that the rest of the inscriptions on the sealed interior hold the secrets to the origins of magic. When you first attune to this artifact, you can choose any Standard or Basic spell to learn instantly as an Arcane History spell instead of its original field, but all its effects remain the same. You can also learn any Advanced spell instantly as an Arcane History spell, but every time you use it, you must tap into this artifact’s power and roll Comprehend, DC17 or you are unable to cast it. You can only use these spells as long as you have the artifact equipped.

NAME: BEACON OF COMMAND
SUBJECT: DIVINATION
DESCRIPTION: A small, intricate magic object like a twisted infinity symbol, representing the eternal nature of duty and responsibility. When you first attune to this artifact, work out with the GM what it wants from you, whether to have you complete a mission, fulfill some greater purpose, improve in some meaningful way, or something else. While this artifact is equipped, you may roll Concentration, DC5 and obtain a Directive, a request from the artifact that furthers its ask of you. For each degree of success, you can add a +1 to checks to complete your Directive. Once you complete your directive, the GM may select a relevant Ability for you to roll to improve. You cannot use this ability again until you complete the Directive.

NAME: SURGING FLASH
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A small sigil forged in iron that hangs from a chain, unremarkable until it activates and starts to glow in a brilliant color, the exact color unique to each user. Whenever you roll for Action, you can activate this artifact and roll Endurance, DC15. On success, take +2AP on top of whatever you rolled for Action, and your next Advanced spell cast in the Action scene has 0 material cost, as explosive higher powers awaken inside the sigil. In addition, while this artifact is equipped, any Evocation spells to move you, such as Flight or Stormstep, take 2 extra degrees of success.

NAME: FAR REALM SONG
SUBJECT: TELEPORTATION
DESCRIPTION: A small flutelike instrument that shimmers with extraplanar magic. While this artifact is equipped, you are immune to extraplanar stress, and if you know the spell Plane Walk, it takes a +2 while this artifact is equipped. You can play the instrument to activate its power anytime you are on a plane other than your own, calling up a haunting, otherworldly song. Roll Art, DC17. On success, you bond with the current plane, allowing you to teleport to it at any time by using this artifact and rolling Art, DC17 again, and teleporting back by using this artifact without a check. While in your target plane, you can render up to 4 people of your choice immune to the extraplanar stress of that plane, and creatures of that plane will regard you with familiarity. You can unbind yourself from a plane at any time or use this artifact to bind to another plane, but if you have it bound to multiple, then when the artifact backfires, it teleports you to a random one of them.

NAME: ARCHITECT OF FATE
SUBJECT: ARCANE HISTORY
DESCRIPTION: A double-ended scroll that unrolls to a shimmering magic canvas that seems to constantly be moving in ways you can’t quite pin down. While you have this artifact equipped, every morning, the GM will give you a piece of information about the overarching storyline or about the day to come, as the canvas describes details of fate. You can spend an hour to roll Art, DC12 and weave your own design into the canvas. For each degree of success, you can decide one of the following details of a spell to be cast soon: • Who will cast it • Where it will be cast • What spell it will be • When it will be cast • What it will be cast on • Fate will conspire to make your design come to pass. It will typically happen within 24 hours, but if you make a very specific request or a difficult or unlikely one, it may take longer. If it does not happen within a week, fate weaves over your design, and the fated spell does not take place.

NAME: CLOAK OF THE SERPENT
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A dark cloak with a deep violet lining inside that shimmers different colors at different angles like the mythic scales of the legendary Abyssal Serpent of the demonic realm. While this artifact is equipped, you are aware of anybody attempting to cast divination on you, although you don’t know their identity. When this happens, you can roll Analyze, DC14 to tap into the demonic magic of the cloak, and for each degree of success, you can take one of the following against the person casting divination on you: • Learn their identity • Force their spell to fail • Force them to Resist • Learn what spell they are casting • Cast a Demonology spell you know on them no matter where they are • Each one of these cloaks is linked to a specific demon. When you first attune to this artifact, the GM will tell you about the demon, including its twisted motive. While this artifact is equipped, you can communicate freely with the demon at any time, possibly getting information from it if you can make it align with the demon’s interests.

NAME: GLIMMERVEIL CHARM
SUBJECT: ENCHANTMENT
DESCRIPTION: A tourmaline pendant with intricate runes along the base, gleaming with a subtle fey enchantment. While this artifact is equipped, people are more likely to like you, trust you, and give you the benefit of the doubt. You can roll Win Over, DC14 to activate the crystal’s magic, and for the next 10 minutes per degree of success, you enter a state of such irresistible charm that anyone attempting to not like you must roll Willpower versus your roll or be charmed. When this effect ends, anyone charmed by you who can see you when the effect ends will become aware that you used magic to influence their feelings, and they tend not to like that. Everyone has feelings about the fey, don’t they?

NAME: SHADOW QUEEN’S ENDLESS SIGHT
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A small crystalline sphere filled with glimmering diamond dust, it stays in place when left in the air, spinning slowly and casting an array of illusory shapes around it. You can use this artifact in two ways: either rolling Hide Object, DC15 to place a tiny sigil in an area or rolling Recall Information, DC15 to activate the sphere and see the sigils you’ve created. When you roll to activate the sphere, you can choose to cast a spell originating from any of the sigils you’ve placed, although you cannot see where you are casting and any targeted spells will hit a random point near the sigil chosen. You can have any number of sigils active, but anyone can make a Search roll against your roll to place a sigil, finding it and, if they choose, destroying it. If they do, you will know the next time you activate the sphere.

NAME: AVIDAR’S CONDUIT
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A heavy, solid capsule charged with magic energy crackling like electricity in the center, sparking more aggressively as more magic builds up. When the conduit is fully charged, you can cast any Advanced spell you know for 0 material cost, expending the charge in the process. Once the charge is spent, you can roll Willpower, DC16 to start its charging process, gathering ambient magic and storing it inside the conduit over a period of time depending on your degrees of success: • 1 degree: 24 hours • 2 degrees: 8 hours • 3 degrees: 4 hours • 4 degrees: 2 hours • 5+ degrees: 1 hour

NAME: BRYNHELM’S RESOLVE
SUBJECT: ALCHEMY
DESCRIPTION: A cold steel sigil you can place anywhere on your body, where it settles in like a tattoo, assisting the user in metabolizing potions. While this artifact is equipped, you can take a +4 to Metabolize checks, and when you roll to Metabolize, you can roll Endurance, DC15 to use this artifact, allowing you to reroll on success. You must take the new roll, but you can use this ability as many times as you like within one roll, the artifact DC increasing by 5 each time. If you roll the minimum time to metabolize a potion while this artifact is equipped, you can take a +4 to your next skill check, as intense magic flows out of it and into you. You can remove the sigil from your body, but once you have, you cannot place it in for another 24 hours.

NAME: LUNAR BUTTERFLY
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A butterfly charm that can be worn like a lapel pin, hair ornament, earring, or similar, where it gleams with a faint twinkling of magic that makes you look more desirable to people. While this artifact is equipped, people will find you more attractive, and if you fail on a Win Over check, people are less likely to be upset. When you fail a Charm check, you can use this artifact and roll Win Over + 6 versus the same DC, potentially succeeding on the check but doing whatever it is via flirtation or general charm.

NAME: GREAT DRAGON’S GUIDANCE
SUBJECT: DRACONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A delicate golden bangle engraved with ancient draconic symbols, tapping into the ancient original magics of dragonkind. When you attempt to learn a spell, you can roll Research, DC14 to draw from the draconological power in the artifact, reducing the material cost to learn the spell by 100 per degree of success (minimum 0) and taking a +1 to the spell-learning roll for each degree of success. You can also roll Research, DC25 while performing a Downtime option other than spell-learning to attempt to learn a spell while performing your other Downtime option chosen, but it costs 100 materials if you fail. The most important part of the artifact, of course, is that it’s very stylish. The sleek design makes it work in any professional setting while still being a kickass magic dragon device. That’s win-win artificy.

NAME: TRUNDALE’S FORK
SUBJECT: TELEKINESIS
DESCRIPTION: A small magic object that looks, honestly, underwhelmingly, like a generic stainless steel dessert fork. While this artifact is equipped, you know if any door is locked just by looking at it, and you can tell if it’s a regular lock, a magical lock, or an electronic lock. For any regular, non-electronic lock, you can roll Sleight of Hand, DC10 to distantly control the lock with the fork, turning it like a key, to lock or unlock the door without anybody being able to notice. For a magical or electronic lock, the DC becomes 20 or equal to the spell check used to create the magic seal, if applicable. You can use this at any distance so long as you can see the door, even if using amplified vision like binoculars or divination, but not through electronic recreations like a video camera.

NAME: SNEAKSWAP
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A puppet in the shape of a cute cartoon ghost that feels like it’s made of plush black material, it does not look like an artifact. You can activate it, rolling Sleight of Hand, DC16, and on success, it transforms into an illusory copy of yourself for 8 hours or until psychically commanded to stop and, if you have an ability to take on somebody else’s appearance like Glamor or Face Stealer, you take a +6 to use it if you use it at the same time, which makes it last for the same 8 hours or until dispelled. Once this artifact is activated, you can give it instructions that it will faithfully follow until its transformed time runs out, at which point it disappears and reappears back by your side. It has only 1 Resolve and rolls 0/5, and cannot handle complex tasks, but it is a perfect replica of your appearance that will behave naturally, and anyone who suspects it’s not you must roll Perception, DC20 to figure out that it’s an illusion.

NAME: SYLENE’S ARMY
SUBJECT: WANDCRAFTING
DESCRIPTION: A small chest with fine detailed designs in gold and red velvet that contains a collection of tiny clockwork figures that emerge to perform wandcrafting work. Whenever you go to sleep, you can roll Concentration, DC15, and on success, you can open the chest and set the figures to work on a wand overnight, allowing you to make any Wandcrafting roll to repair or create a wand in your sleep. This roll does not take any regular Wandcrafting bonuses you might have, including any bonuses from knowing the target well, but it does take a +1 for each degree of success on the artifact roll. Every Sylene’s Army has one clockwork figure called Poppy who can’t seem to do anything right, so any time you make a wand with this, there’s always one goofy little detail that looks a little bit off on the wand. That’s Poppy’s doing. Sylene never figured out how to deal with the Poppy problem.

NAME: TIMEFOLD RINGS
SUBJECT: CHRONOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A pair of rings, one red and one gold, that glow faintly when both are worn together. While this artifact is equipped, casting spells as rituals only takes 30 minutes, as the magic bends time around the precise movements of the magic. You can roll Concentration, DC14 to activate more of the rings’ magic to subtract 5 minutes per degree of success (minimum 1 minute) and take a +1 to the spell for each degree of success, but the artifact has a strange design quirk that nobody’s managed to work out: whenever you use it this way, you get a taste of hard-boiled egg in your mouth. Experienced users can’t even taste eggs anymore, they’re so inured to it.

NAME: NEPHERA’S BRANCH OF HARMONY
SUBJECT: HERBALISM
DESCRIPTION: A gnarled staff made of promiseblossom, a rare tree with deep magic properties to bring one closer to nature’s magic. While you are attuned to this artifact, your magic plant cultivation capacity increases to 150 * your Herbalism ranks. In addition, you can roll Tracking, DC12 to gain one clue per degree of success about the location of the nearest magic plant, or to identify any magic plant.

NAME: TRUNDALE’S TRAPDOOR
SUBJECT: TELEPORTATION
DESCRIPTION: A thin string that glimmers in different prismatic colors, you can pull it to stretch it out to a dark cloth that seems to swallow up anything it comes into contact with. When someone doesn’t know you’re there, you can roll Stealth, DC15 to slip the cloth over their face. On success, they must roll Perception or Agility versus your check or be captured in the cloth, which drops them into a tiny subspace pocket for 1 minute per degree of failure on their check. They are unconscious during this time, and when the effect ends, they are dropped on the ground where they originally were, dazed and confused as if waking up from a deep sleep. Even if you have an effect that would normally let you use items at a distance, you must be in direct contact with the artifact to use it, unless you have the Artificer rank ability Twin Spirits on this artifact.

NAME: REDMARE’S POWER FLAME
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A lens worn like a contact over one eye that glows with an intricate swirling magic design. While this artifact is equipped, rolling a critical success on a spell adds 1 degree of success to the roll as the magic surges up from the artifact and adds to your spell power. You can also roll Willpower, DC17 after rolling a spell to add 1 to the check per degree of success, although any backfires or improvement rolls are triggered with the original roll, not the modified roll.

NAME: YAZIR’S STOCKPILE
SUBJECT: WANDCRAFTING
DESCRIPTION: A simple, brassy plate that slowly coalesces magic into materials. While this artifact is attuned to you, it collects 250 materials per day, although they aren’t suitable to be collected as regular magic materials. When you roll to create or repair a wand, you can roll Improvise, DC16 and use the stored materials from this artifact instead of spending your own. You can always tell when a wandcrafter has one of these, because they make a lot of wands they don’t need. You can really, really tell when a wandcrafter has two of these.

NAME: AELYN’S CRYSTAL BAND
SUBJECT: CRYSTALLOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A stylish black band worn on the arm that refracts a thousand faces of a crystal aligned with it. While this artifact is equipped, you can roll any Crystallomancy spell to Align to a given crystal targeting this band instead of yourself. When you do, instead of rolling to Resist, roll for this artifact to backfire. You take any benefits of alignment this artifact has as long as it is equipped, as well as any of your own. If you and the band are Aligned to the same crystal type, then you take the effect of whichever has more degrees of success.

NAME: MIRROR OF STRANGE FACES
SUBJECT: SUMMONING
DESCRIPTION: An ornate mirror in an unusual, twisted design, allowing you to see reflections of other planes. While this artifact is equipped, you can name a plane and see a glimpse of it, although you cannot decide where in the plane you can see.
