You can roll Persuasion, DC17 and name a type of extraplanar creature to activate the mirror’s magic, and on success, you link with that type of extraplanar creature and can form a connection with it through the mirror, communicating with it as if in the same space. If the creature is willing or if it fails a Strong roll against your check, it becomes friendly to you and forms a regular link, allowing you to commune with that creature at any point without a check and take a +6 to any checks to summon it. You can link with any number of extraplanar creatures this way. In addition, golems can be confused with the mirror: roll Persuasion versus their Weak roll, leaving them confused and dazed on a success.

NAME: ONOMY’S STONE SHAPER
SUBJECT: TRANSMUTATION
DESCRIPTION: A twelve-sided chamber with sigils on each side and an interplanar pocket on the inside, glowing with a transmutative energy. While this artifact is active, you can put any object under 100 grams into the chamber and instantly reform it into a different shape, of the same mass and material. You can also roll Improvise, DC14 to place up to 10 kilograms per degree of success into the chamber to be reformed into whatever shape you like, no matter how complex, over the course of 10 minutes. You can also choose for it to come out as a different material or a combination of different materials, although you can’t change it into anything too different, but it takes 1 hour instead if you do. Despite the name, you can put any inorganic object into it to transmute, but Starfall transmutation professor Onomy really likes rocks, so here we are.

NAME: SILENCE’S RAZOR SIGHT
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A blood-red eyepatch, covering one eye with a glowing red eye design that matches the Great Eagle’s eagle-eye design. While this artifact is equipped, it guides your vision and helps you analyze your magical mistakes, and you can take a +6 to any Body or Sleight of Hand checks to physically throw an object with precision. When you fail a spell check by 1 degree of success, you can roll Analyze, DC19, and on success, you can choose to either turn the roll into 1 degree of success, or to trigger an improvement roll in the spell subject. You can only use this ability if the original roll is 1 degree of failure, not a roll modified beyond roll bonuses.

NAME: QUEEN OF THE ARSENAL
SUBJECT: ARTIFICY
DESCRIPTION: A tall, ornate staff wrapped in lunar silver filaments and topped with a bronzite crystal, reaching out to other artifacts. While this artifact is equipped, you have a +4 to all Resist checks from attempting to attune to an artifact, and you can tell what artifacts a person has equipped by looking at them. When you attempt to attune to an artifact, you can roll Willpower, DC5 to channel this artifact’s power into it and take a +1 to the attunement roll for each degree of success on this check.

NAME: CRANE’S EYES
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A diamond-shaped sigil of abyssal steel set with a demonic crystal in the center, that you can press into your body either directly in the center of your collar or on your forehead, where it settles in as a faintly glowing mark. While the artifact is activated in this way, you can tell a person’s total bonus to Resist Doubt just by looking at them. You can roll Persuasion, DC16 to force a target you make eye contact with to roll Willpower versus your check or Resist Doubt, as the artifact seeds demonic magic into their mind to make them question themselves. If they fail with 2 or more degrees of failure on their Willpower check, they don’t realize what you did. You can remove the sigil from your skin at any point, but you must Resist Doubt, DC12 when you do.

NAME: CRYSTAL SHINE OF THE EMPTY MIND
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A small vial of crystal fluid that forms into a shimmering magic dust in the air when poured out, hypnotizing a target. Roll Deception, DC18. On success, one target per degree of success who can see the shimmering cloud must roll Recall Information versus your check or forget the last one minute per degree of failure on their check. In addition, while you have this artifact equipped, you can perfectly remember everything you perceived from the last five minutes, even if you didn’t consciously take it in, so you generally know what you’re erasing from a person’s mind. An artifact cherished and coveted by Rascals, the long and pretentious name puts many of them off, so it’s typically referred to as “Forgettin’ Juice,” much to the artifact designer’s dismay.

NAME: RIVIERA’S BLACK PORTAL
SUBJECT: TELEPORTATION
DESCRIPTION: A beautiful, ornate inkwell that contains a deep, churning liquid darker than black. While this artifact is equipped, you can pour the inkwell out onto a surface, which creates a portal softly radiating darkness. Roll Hide Object, DC14, and on success, you create a secret portal that remains indefinitely or until you close the inkwell again, which causes it to blink shut. Anyone who doesn’t succeed on a Perception or Search check against your check is oblivious to the portal, and if they step on it, they are pulled into a subspace of your design. You know instantly if somebody falls into the portal, no matter where you are, and you can choose to open a communication portal to speak to the person inside the subspace. They remain inside the subspace for 15 minutes or until you close the inkwell, which expels them instantly, returning them to where they fell into the portal. Once you close the inkwell, it takes 10 minutes for it to refill.

NAME: DRAGON’S PRESENCE
SUBJECT: DRACONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A set of red-scaled pauldrons that give you a dragon’s powerful, commanding aura. While this artifact is active, you have a +2 to Willpower, and most people tend to fear you, respect you, or both. You can roll Willpower, DC20 to emit an explosive wave of commanding energy, forcing everybody within 10 meters to roll Willpower versus your check or Resist Fear. For each degree of success past the first on your check, you can choose to double the size of the effect radius. If you use this effect on a draconic creature, they are likely to take it as a challenge and either flee or attack you. If you somehow manage an effect radius larger than 1 kilometer, you attract the attention of full dragons who want to cut you down to size, but if you can manage that, you might be able to take them.

NAME: HAYWORTH’S POISON VEIN
SUBJECT: ALCHEMY
DESCRIPTION: An intricate mechanical piece of magic equipment worn over one arm, pumping with churning magical substances. While this artifact is attuned, you can recognize any poison instantly by looking at it, even if it’s disguised as a different potion. You can roll Improvise, DC10, and on success, you can instantly obtain a poison up to a material cost of 100 per degree of success by expending the material cost on the spot. You can also drink any poison, and choose to break it down instead of metabolizing it, converting it into materials equal to its material cost - 50, but to anyone watching, it will appear as though you are taking the poison normally. You know, in case you want to scare your friends.

NAME: ARCHITECT’S GOLDEN FLOWER
SUBJECT: RUNOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A small, ornate flower made of lunar platinum and inscribed with ancient runic designs. While this artifact is equipped, you can take a +4 to any check to place a Runic Blessing, and you can gain one clue about what any given rune does just by looking at it. When you set out to create a rune, you can place this artifact in the center of the rune as you make it, where it glows and starts floating above the runic surface, empowering more complex runic designs. Roll Analyze, DC15. For each degree of success, you can reduce one glyph in the rune to Intensity Rank 0, although any runes you create this way must still have at least 2 runes at their regular Intensity Rank (for example, on 3 degrees of success, you can ignore 3 glyphs’ Intensity Rank on a 5-glyph rune, but only 2 glyphs’ Intensity Rank on a 4-glyph rune).

NAME: WINGS OF THE FEY WALKER
SUBJECT: TELEPORTATION
DESCRIPTION: A small brace of fey quartz you can place subtly on your upper back, preparing to imbue your teleportation with fey magic. While this artifact is equipped, you can sense anybody teleporting in your vicinity, as well as see anybody hiding in an adjacent plane as a semi-transparent figure. When you roll to teleport within your home plane, you can roll Art, DC10 to activate the wings on this artifact, sprouting a massive set of spectral wings from the brace that leave a shimmering trail behind you. You can add your degrees of success on the artifact check to your degrees of success on your check to teleport. It also makes you crave tarragon. Fey love tarragon.

NAME: MASTER OF SKULLS
SUBJECT: NECROMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A skull-like mask worn over the lower half of your face, channeling necromantic energy. When you confront a body in any form, including at least most of the bones of a creature or a corpse that’s already necromantically animated, you can channel the mask’s commanding force into the corpse and roll Comprehend, DC17 to animate it under your command. If the target is already animated or if it is recently dead, it can choose to resist, rolling Willpower against your check with its current stats or most recent stats it had in life. On failure, or if it consents, the corpse is animated as a creature with stats equal to what it most recently had, whether in life or in undeath, and it remains animated indefinitely and follows your verbal commands. You cannot perceive or command it from a distance, but you can send out a psychic call that it can hear from anywhere to order it to come to you, and you can sense if it is ever destroyed. You can have any number of necromantic creatures animated this way, and you can choose to dispel the magic and destroy them at any point.

NAME: MEGHAN’S MAGICAL RUNE MAGIC FAIRY BLOSSOM
SUBJECT: RUNOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A multicolored flower made of crystals that turn slowly on the stem, each one glowing with a different runic energy. When you first attune to this artifact, you instantly learn 4 Basic Runology spells that give Runic Blessings, and you can tell just by looking if somebody has a Runic Blessing while this artifact is equipped. When you’ve given somebody a Runic Blessing, you can activate this artifact and roll Improvise, DC14, and on success, you can cast a new Runic Blessing that runs simultaneously, the flower lighting up while the Runic Blessing is active. You cannot use this ability again until that second Runic Blessing runs out and the flower loses its magic glow. Unexpected rising-star artificer Meghan Gosling really liked this artifact and wanted it to have a “really extra special name,” and everyone told her it was too long, but she kind of does her own thing. Most people refer to this artifact as Meghan’s Blossom. Just don’t let Meghan catch you doing it.

NAME: SONGWON’S SAVAGE STEP
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A set of bands in jet-black with crackling golden topaz worn up the length of both arms and around both ankles, all of which radiate brilliant magic light while you run. While this artifact is equipped, you run at twice your regular speed and jump twice your regular height, giving a +4 to Athletics checks to run and jump, and you can roll Endurance, DC16 to spend 1 minute per degree of success running and jumping at 8 times your regular abilities, giving a +10 to Athletics checks to run and jump, although you make an incredible amount of noise and light while this is active. Most human bodies are not designed to handle those speeds: depending on the type of movement you attempt to make, the GM may ask you to roll Agility to avoid crashing and being forced to Resist.

NAME: CLOUDLESS’ CLOAK OF STARS
SUBJECT: CHRONOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A rather stylish artifact that looks like a light beige scarf but billows out to a spectral cloak lined with a glittering starscape when activated. While this artifact is equipped, you can activate its magic, surrounding you with shimmering chronomantic energy. Roll Willpower, DC15. On success, you can pin the current moment in time for 1 minute per degree of success, and once the time runs out or earlier if you choose, you revert time to the point you activated the cloak. When you revert time, you clear any stacks of Wound obtained during that time, but other condition stacks remain.

NAME: SPELL-LOCK MATRIX
SUBJECT: CRYSTALLOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A crystal matrix of four glimmering crystal cores set anywhere on your body, glowing with thin magic lines of pure colored light between them. While this artifact is equipped, you can cast a spell on any one of the crystals to load the spell into that crystal. Any material costs, backfires, improvements, ritual time, or other aspects of casting are resolved at the time of casting, storing the spell inside any of the four crystals. At any point while a crystal has a spell loaded, you can cast that spell with a roll equal to the roll it was loaded with, which costs 1AP if used in action, no matter the spell level. If you have this artifact equipped and you see somebody else with this artifact equipped, you can instantly tell what spells they have loaded, so it can be kind of a bonding moment when people see each other rocking this, unless one of them is loaded up on hexes, which gets awkward real quick.

NAME: MEYRI’S PAINTBRUSH
SUBJECT: SUMMONING
DESCRIPTION: A paintbrush with a beautiful passionoak handle banded in brassy gold and bristles harvested from a far-flung extraplanar space, capable of manipulating the fabric of reality. You can roll Art, DC10 to paint with the paintbrush, creating the shape of an object or small creature, which summons the painted object or a golem in the shape of the painted creature into being. You can create 1 kilogram of object, a number which doubles for each degree of success past the first. The brush then takes 1 hour to regain the extraplanar material to paint with, but you can spend 100 materials to instantly recharge it. While you are equipped to the artifact, you also have the undying adoration of blue fuzzies, small extraplanar creatures that look a little like living pom-poms. You should be careful around them. You kind of can’t shake them once you have them.

NAME: GARLYTH’S BURNING FLOWER
SUBJECT: ALCHEMY
DESCRIPTION: A thin, ornate paper that you can mentally command at any moment to unfold itself into a brilliant lotus shape, and a spectral flame erupts in the center. While this artifact is equipped, you can take a +4 to all Metabolize checks. You can activate the artifact’s spectral flame and pour a potion into it, rolling Improvise, DC20. On success, you can gain the effects of the potion for 5 minutes per degree of success, or the maximum duration of the potion, whichever is shorter, without needing to metabolize. At the end of this duration, you crash, experiencing the opposite effect of the potion, as determined by the GM. You can use this ability even while currently metabolizing a potion. You can mentally command the flower to close again at any time, but if you open it and don’t feed it a potion, it will be upset.

NAME: HEART OF SHATTERED GLASS
SUBJECT: DIVINATION
DESCRIPTION: A heart-shaped locket made of crystal that seems to have a reflection of a different person in each of its countless faces. While this artifact is equipped, you always know if you’re being perceived with divination or if divinatory perception is close by. You can open the artifact and roll Concentration or Comprehend, DC20, causing a number of crystal fragments equal to your degrees of success to tumble out, swirling slowly through the air around you. You can touch any one fragment and either send it to embed itself into a person’s soul, or use it to cast Scry on a person without material cost. If you have embedded a fragment into a person’s soul before, you can choose to take the result of your artifact roll on the Scry check against that person, and it cannot be tracked. The fragment embedding can be felt with a similar sensation as being pinched, but otherwise the target won’t necessarily know what you’ve done. The swirling crystal fragments remain for 5 minutes, after which they fall to the ground and disappear.

NAME: NAIL OF THE GREAT DRAGON
SUBJECT: DRACONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: An ornate, twisted bolt from the same metal as the great magic vault that once sealed away the Great Dragon of Starfall Academy, the seal whose shattering represents the truce between humans and dragons. While this artifact is equipped, draconic creatures will not be hostile to you without some particular grievance against you specifically, and you can make Charm checks on any draconic creature as on a person. You can activate it in the presence of a draconic creature and roll Willpower or Win Over, DC20, and if the creature is hostile to you, it will become neutral, and if the creature is not hostile, you can forge a telepathic bond between you and the draconic creature that persists over any distance on the same plane, although it can be broken at any point either you or the creature desires to break it. When you have a link to a draconic creature, take a +6 to Charm checks against it.

NAME: MIND MURMUR
SUBJECT: ARCANE HISTORY
DESCRIPTION: A flower-shaped ornament just big enough to fit in your palm, each petal a different crystal imbued with a different aspect of cryptomancy. While this artifact is equipped, you can take a +4 to any Persuasion roll that you spend at least 5 minutes of conversation on, as it weaves secret messages into your words. You can activate the artifact by rolling Tact against the Comprehend of a target you are in conversation with, and on a success, you can transmit a subliminal message underneath your words that will emerge in their mind at some point over the next 4 hours as a thought from their own mind, although they will not necessarily entertain the thought. On three or more degrees of failure, the target knows what you’re doing. You can choose any number of targets to explicitly hear the subliminal messages.

NAME: CRYSTAL GUARDIAN
SUBJECT: CRYSTALLOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A small, unremarkable sphere of reflective metal. While this artifact is equipped, you can instantly tell what crystal any given person is aligned to just by looking, if any. You can activate the artifact by rolling Tracking, DC20. On success, the sphere expands into an array of spectral crystals that swirl like stars around you. You can choose one target per degree of success who you can see or whose full name you know, and they must roll Willpower or Stealth or be invisibly marked. You can expand the sphere at any time without a check and instantly learn the location of everybody you’ve marked with this artifact, as long as they’re on your current plane. You can only have up to 6 people marked in this way, but you can unmark anybody at any point. Good for international super spies or campus busybodies alike.

NAME: SARINA’S SONG
SUBJECT: HYPNOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A set of four rings worn in any configuration across both hands, in a shimmery rose-gold that seems to always refract a slightly different color than before. While this artifact is active, you can touch anybody experiencing a dream and see the dream through their eyes, and you can take a +4 to Hypnomancy spells to affect their dream. You can activate the artifact by rolling Deception, DC21, to speak laced with hypnomantic effect. Anybody who listens to it for more than one minute must roll Willpower or Endurance against your check or fall into a trance for 10 minutes where they experience a dream of anything you like. A target who experiences the full 10 minutes of this trance dream must roll Comprehend versus your check or be unable to tell which parts from the last hour, including the dream, were real and which were a dream. The titular hypnomancer herself, Sarina, got in trouble during her scholarship at Starfall for using this artifact during class presentations, but she always thought it was funny. To be fair, it kind of was.

NAME: GREAT BOAR’S ASPECT
SUBJECT: BESTIOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A pin emblazoned with a glowing emerald sigil. While this artifact is equipped, you can lift and carry up to ten times your weight without a check, and you can take a +6 to Athletics or Endurance checks to hold or carry a large weight. You can activate the artifact by rolling Creature, DC21, to remove the pin and throw it forward, where it expands into a creature that takes whatever form you specify and fits within a 2-meter cube. The creature lasts for 1 hour or until you dismiss it, and it has 1 Resolve and rolls 0/15 regardless of its form, but the form you choose will inform what skills it is strong in. The creature follows your commands as best it can, but it is known for being a little pigheaded no matter if its head is pig or not. It always wants to be glorious.

NAME: IMPERIA’S INFINITY
SUBJECT: WANDCRAFTING
DESCRIPTION: A pendant with a small crystal infinity symbol that, while worn, creates a spectral swirl of silvery magic that flows like an infinity symbol around you. While this artifact is equipped, you can equip wands that are bound to somebody else. You can activate the artifact by touching the pendant and speaking a name you bestow on it upon first attuning, and rolling Comprehend, DC19. For each degree of success, you can equip an additional wand past your first for the next 10 minutes. At the end of this time, you return to only the first wand you had equipped. You cannot equip multiple of the same wand with this ability. This isn’t because of limitations in the artifact or because of unstable magic power, but because wands are total divas and couldn’t bear having copies.

NAME: WARPDROP
SUBJECT: ALCHEMY
DESCRIPTION: A band of feytouched lunar platinum worn around the upper arm, with six slots for your potion vials, where they disappear into magic glyphs on the band once potions are slotted in. While this artifact is equipped, you can take a potion or give a potion to a willing target subtly by touching the artifact and whispering a signal phrase to teleport the potion into the target’s system (1AP). If the target is unwilling, roll Concentration against their Perception (2AP). If they succeed, the potion appears in their mouth, and they can choose whether to take it or spit it out. You cannot transmit anything other than potions to people with the artifact, in an effort to keep people from giving orange juice to the fey.

NAME: SHARD OF SPLINTERED SOUL
SUBJECT: NECROMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A glassy orb that contains a shard of angel’s stone inside, a volatile and rare compound said to stabilize your soul—and a shard destabilizes it. While this artifact is equipped, you have no magic presence, and runes do not respond to your presence. You can clasp the orb in both hands and speak an incantation to activate the artifact, rolling Concentration or Stealth, DC20. On success, you transfer your soul out of your body and into a target object you can see, where it moves as if enchanted under your command. Your senses while in this state depend on the object you transferred to, but you generally have at least limited, murky sight and a sense of touch. Your body remains animate and can follow simple instructions you left for it, but it cannot speak. You can return to your body at any point, at which point you instantly become aware of everything your body sensed since you left it. If you like disassociating, this is basically the next level.

NAME: CLAY OF THE CRYSTAL CREATOR
SUBJECT: ENCHANTMENT
DESCRIPTION: A silvery bangle inlaid with a long, thin band of what first appears like crystal but on closer inspection is a flowing translucent fluid. While this artifact is equipped, you can immediately tell when an object is enchanted, the basic details of what its enchantment does, and you can place the same enchantment on other objects as if you know the spell as long as you can see that enchanted object. You can tap two fingers to the center of the bangle to draw out the fluid into a claylike compound that molds under your will. Roll Art, DC21. On success, you can instantly create an object up to 1 kilogram made entirely of this material, although it can make itself look like anything at all. This object is automatically enchanted with any Standard spell you know or any Basic spell you like. The object lasts as long as you like, but you cannot use this ability again until you dismiss it to return it to your bangle.

NAME: EYE OF THE WISE DRAGON
SUBJECT: DRACONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A piece of jewelry that may take different forms but is always set with a crystal dragon’s eye forged from a willingly-given scale of a water wyrm. While this artifact is active, you can take a +4 to any one spell of your choosing when you first attune to this artifact. You can roll Concentration, DC23 to add this bonus to any spell you cast. Intended as a symbol of harmony between humans and dragons, this artifact entered the public awareness when it was used by Levinia Argon, who tricked a water wyrm and then used the artifact to crush her enemies, which kind of got in the way of the whole harmony thing.

NAME: ALIRA’S THOUSAND EYES
SUBJECT: TELEKINESIS
DESCRIPTION: A small device of crimson blinkstone crystal set in a base of abyssal steel. While this artifact is equipped, you can cast Telekinesis spells without a wand with no penalty, and you can take a +6 to checks to cast them subtly. You can activate this artifact by spending 5 minutes performing a ritual on the gem and rolling Hide Object, DC22, to create a Control Node, a tiny, invisible magic tag in the target location. Any time you have at least one Control Node on the same plane as you, you can touch the gemstone to create swirling orbits of magic around you representing your Control Nodes, and you can see from and cast any Telekinesis spell you know from the location of a Control Node instead of your current location. You can only have a maximum number of Control Nodes equal to your Telekinesis rank (minimum 3), but you can dispel any of them from anywhere. A powerful artifact, its most common use is for subterfuge, followed closely by as a baby monitor.

NAME: MARTELO’S WRATH
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A half mask of glass forged in draconic fire that melds with your face when you put it on, leaving only Martelo’s symbol glowing like a rune over your skin. While this artifact is active, any time you succeed on casting a spell against a target’s roll, add an extra degree of success. You can activate the artifact by rolling Endurance, DC24. On success, the mask erupts with a flame of magic energy that courses through your body, empowering your casting magic. Choose one field of magic. For the next 10 minutes, you can take a +6 to all spells cast in this field, but you cannot cast any other field of magic. You can end the effect early, but whenever it ends, you crash for 5 minutes and cannot cast any spells until the crash wears off. Legendary in the mythos of Baranomichi Colégio, great evoker Martelo was known for breaking things, hitting things, and… and breaking things.

NAME: SILVER WATER MIRROR
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A ring of lunar silver with a spectral mirror in the middle that seems to ripple like water. While this artifact is active, you can cast the spell Second Self as a Basic spell, whether you know it or not. You can hold the ring up to capture somebody’s reflection, lighting up stars and galaxies along the outer ring, and roll Analyze or Read Person, DC23. On success, you store a likeness of the target person in the Mirror, and any time you use the artifact’s ability to cast Second Self, you can expend one captured likeness to summon a spectral copy of that person. You can choose for the copy to follow your directions or to behave with the original target’s personality and mannerisms, to help it potentially pass as the target. Depending on how guarded the target is and how many degrees of success you got on your artifact activation roll, the GM may decide the spectral copy has information the target has, as well. Good to let you hang out with your unattainable K-Pop idol crush.

NAME: GOODBYE BLOSSOM
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A cherry branch that continually sheds spectral cherry blossoms, disappearing before they touch the ground. While this artifact is equipped, you can take a +4 to all Illusion spells cast while undetected. You can activate the artifact by rolling Concentration or Stealth, DC20. On success, you can cast Invisibility and Nondetection as Basic spells for the next 1 minute, whether you know them or not, as the blossom sheds a haunting violet light that only you can see, swathing you in magic of disappearance. Once popular with rascals, the artifact became too well-known when a mainstream (read: sellout) spectral rocker started wearing it to shows and Buzzfeed called it “the ultimate emo kid’s dream.” That kind of killed its cool cred.

NAME: MASTER OF WORLDS
SUBJECT: TELEPORTATION
DESCRIPTION: A pair of spectral boots worn over your own shoes that flare up with a ghostly lavender flame. While this artifact is equipped, you cannot be teleported against your will, and any time you’re on a plane other than your own, you can always choose to instantly return to your own. You can activate the artifact by stomping one foot down and calling up a shower of ghostly flame. Roll Willpower or Improvise, DC21. On success, you create a temporary subspace you can bring yourself into and up to 4 other people close by who don’t make a Willpower check against your artifact roll. You can determine the general nature of the subspace, but the details will be out of your control except on higher degrees of success. You can stay in this subspace indefinitely, but anybody else in the subspace can roll to escape every 10 minutes, as long as they’re able to cast magic.

NAME: TIMESONG ORCHESTRA
SUBJECT: CHRONOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A baton that at first glance looks like a simple wand, made from ancient whisperwood rich with chronomantic power. While this artifact is equipped, Advanced Chronomancy spells have their Material cost halved. You can hold the baton up in the air to lead the weaves of the timestream like players in an orchestra, rolling Art or Comprehend, DC24. On success, you can get a brief overview of the important moments from the past 24 hours of a given target you can see, and you can revert time to any of those important moments. You stay in the past for 15 minutes per degree of success on your roll, and you return to the position you were in at that time, although you gain one clue about the target’s location or what they’re doing per degree of success. Any changes you make during this time remain in the timestream.

NAME: CELESTIAL GUARDIAN
SUBJECT: RESTORATION
DESCRIPTION: An instrument made of Pegasus crystal, a precious and rare glassy magic substance that looks like a frozen lake at dawn. While this artifact is equipped, beasts are usually docile towards you as long as you don’t harm them first, and you can take a +4 to any check to contest a demonic effect. You can blow a note on the instrument to roll Creature, DC21, and summon a spectral creature that protects and empowers a target. A protected target cannot be affected by demons or any hexes cast while they’re protected, and they take a +4 to two Abilities of your choice. If they are forced to Resist, they can choose to instead have the creature absorb the magic and disappear. The creature will take a different form to suit the abilities it is empowering, and the artifact itself can be any instrument that is blown into to play. Some are ocarinas, some are pan flutes, and there has been a recorded instance of one being a kazoo.

NAME: ZAGOBAR’S PROMISE
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A ribbon the color of dried blood tied somewhere on your person, made with an impossibly soft and always-warm silk from the abyssal fireworm. While this artifact is equipped, you can instantly tell by looking at someone how much room you take up in their thoughts, which also includes whether they’re aware of your presence. When somebody is aware of your presence, you can look them in the eye and speak a phrase chosen when you first attune to this artifact, rolling Stealth or Deception against their Concentration or Comprehend. On success, you briefly scramble their awareness, and they are no longer aware of your presence for the next 30 seconds, acting as though you aren’t there. If they don’t perceive you at the end of the 30 seconds, they remain unaware of your presence. As an upside, the ribbon is also pretty cute. It looks great on a bag handle or even tied into your hair. Thanks, Zagobar, Choking King of Despair!

NAME: HEARTFEEDER
SUBJECT: SUMMONING
DESCRIPTION: A small amulet whose design emulates the pink swirl, an extraplanar creature famous for feeding off the awareness of people. The artifact links with your body, interplanar communications sigils lighting up over large portions of your body as you use it, and while it is equipped, you can always tell what emotions someone is experiencing, even if they’re hiding it, giving a +4 to relevant Read Person and Tact checks. You can channel the artifact’s magic to light up sigils over your body and roll Improvise or Comprehend, DC20. For each degree of success, one target of your choice must roll Willpower or Comprehend against your check or have one emotion you name replaced with another emotion in equal amounts, as the artifact channels extraplanar creatures that feed on given emotions and metabolize them into others. The effect is only a shock change—if somebody is angry at you for a situation and you replace that anger without changing the situation, they will likely become angry again over the situation. So, you know, you still have to work at your relationships.

NAME: SONGWON’S MIRROR
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A jet-black folding mirror lined with abyssal ruby and pixie blood glass that shows reflections of viewers represented as water sprites. You can roll Research, DC17 to study the reflection of a person in the mirror, storing their likeness as a reflection that stays in the mirror regardless of who’s looking. The Mirror can hold any number of likenesses this way, and you can use it without a check to summon the likeness as a water sprite. The water sprite disguises itself as a perfect copy of the likeness, including the ability to perfectly mimic the target’s voice and mannerisms, and obeys your spoken instructions to the best of its ability. It has 5 Resolve and rolls 5/13, but it cannot restore Resolve. If you reduce a person to 0 Resolve while you have their likeness summoned as a water sprite, you can roll Willpower versus their Willpower, and on success, you can seal them inside the Mirror, only being released when you choose to or when this artifact loses its attunement.

NAME: HAUNTING SIGHT
SUBJECT: WANDCRAFTING
DESCRIPTION: A silken black glove woven with fey magic that hums softly when listened to closely. You can touch a wand and roll Perception, DC15 to plant Wandsight into that wand, a subtle effect that cannot be traced back to you. Anytime you have Wandsight placed on any wand, you can place the glove over your eyes and roll Perception, DC15 to perceive everything around any wand you’ve placed Wandsight on for as long as you like, but you are oblivious to your own surroundings. While perceiving a wand’s surroundings in this way, you perceive everything as if you were at the wand’s location yourself, and your observation cannot be detected. The ultimate expression of twisted wandcrafters, because when wandcrafters turn evil, they don’t hurt people, they just become voyeurs.

NAME: BLACKWING PROMISE
SUBJECT: DIVINATION
DESCRIPTION: A band worn over one shoulder in abyssal black quartz, always warm to the touch and subtly shimmering at the edges. You can roll Read Person, DC20 to summon a set of black spectral wings from your back and a spectral eye mask, that lasts for 5 minutes or until you dispel it. While this effect is active, anyone who meets your gaze must roll Willpower versus your check, and on failure, you can read their thoughts, starting with surface-level thoughts and reaching deeper thoughts and subconscious feelings and beliefs with more degrees of success. The dramatic visibility of the effect really gets in the way of what could be a powerful subtle technique, but hexologists are all less in it for the power and more as a cry for attention, after all.

NAME: HEAVENLY SCRIBE
SUBJECT: ENCHANTMENT
DESCRIPTION: A swirl of fey quartz dust suspended in a living fluid substance that moves constantly in intricate, flowing shapes in the air. When someone watches the Scribe move, you can roll Persuasion, DC17, and on success, they must roll Recall Information versus your check or let you rewrite one of their memories per degree of failure on their check. These false memories return to normal after 24 hours, but you can spend 3 degrees of failure on their check to rewrite one of their memories permanently. You can also spend a degree of failure on their check to force them to Resist Doubt either right away or at some point in the next 24 hours.

NAME: JACIARA’S BLOOD EXCHANGE
SUBJECT: RUNOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A set of intricate black-and-red rings crafted from the bent metal of an abyssal cage, set into the palm of each hand, where they course with magic pulses around the lengths of them. While this artifact is equipped, you can take a +6 to Runology, and whenever you make a rune, you can activate the artifact by rolling Willpower, DC25. On success, you can draw the life force out of yourself or someone you can see to power the rune. For each degree of success, one person of your choice (which can be the same one multiple times) must Resist, DC20. For each Resist roll you force this way, the material cost to create the rune is reduced by 500 (minimum 0) and 5 hours of the rune creation time is instantly completed.

NAME: SILENCE’S ARMORY
SUBJECT: ARTIFICY
DESCRIPTION: An unremarkable black bangle worn over one wrist that, once worn by the person attuned to it, expands to cover the arm and shoulder with a glimmering black metallic material that is nearly unbreakable. While this artifact is equipped, any Athletics or Endurance check that relies on holding something steady with the arm this artifact is on take a +8 bonus, and you can lay a hand on a person and roll Analyze, DC19. On a success, the Armory gains a glyph on its surface that glows in a pale light, and at any point, you can tap this glyph to summon a weapon or poison specifically designed to prey on the weaknesses of the target person. If you attack the target with the weapon or if they take the poison, they must Resist twice. You can dispel the summoned weapon or poison at any time, but the glyph is used up on summoning and is not returned on dispelling.

NAME: WREATH OF VENOM
SUBJECT: ALCHEMY
DESCRIPTION: A ring of cold black abyssal iron that floats like a halo at the back of your head. While this artifact is equipped, you can take a +6 to Metabolize rolls, as the artifact’s magic pulses through your system, filtering potions’ essential energies. You can take any poison and roll Endurance, DC21, to activate the artifact, where it lights up with a pale blue glow, connecting across the interplanar continuum to a target. For each degree of success, you can choose one person you’ve seen in the last 24 hours to roll Endurance against your check or also receive the results of the poison you took. On three or more degrees of success, you are unaffected by the poison.

NAME: TRADER OF BONE AND BLOOD
SUBJECT: SUMMONING
DESCRIPTION: A small old pocketbook bound in bone-white leather that floats at the ready, and spills open to reveal sheets and sheets of intricate logbooks detailing interplanar trades. While this artifact is active, you can take a +4 to any Charm checks used in bartering and trade negotiations, and you can activate the artifact by rolling Creature, DC23. On success, one person of your choice must roll Willpower or Endurance versus your spell check or be sent to a different plane and have their place traded with a creature of your choice native to that plane, any creature with a maximum Resolve less than or equal to the target’s current Resolve. This creature is not necessarily friendly to you but is at least generally cooperative, and it can disguise itself as the target person, although only visually. This trade lasts for 10 minutes/1 hour/8 hours/24 hours on 1/2/3/4+ degrees of success.

NAME: SACRIFICIAL NAIL
SUBJECT: EVOCATION
DESCRIPTION: A jagged old nail made of abyssal iron, constantly ice-cold to the touch. You can drive the nail into a person, including yourself, and roll Willpower, DC12. An unwilling target can roll Agility or Endurance versus your check to escape the effect. If you succeed, you force the target to Resist, DC17 and take a +4 bonus to your next spell, with an extra +1 for each degree of success past the first on your Willpower check.

NAME: GRIEF BLOOM
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A black flower that settles onto the skin like a tattoo waiting to be used. You can touch the tattoo and roll Concentration, DC10 to have the flower’s petals fall off and disappear, and one target you can see must roll Willpower versus your check or be afflicted with one point of Grief per degree of failure on their check, forcing them to Resist Loss against your check once for each point of Grief at some point over the next 24 hours, over which time the flower’s petals regrow. You cannot use this artifact again until the petals have regrown. Unless the target succeeds on their Willpower check with 3 or more degrees of success, they will not notice the effect.

NAME: LAKE OF THE CRANE
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A ring in the shape of a simple elegant band in a soft, muted gold. While this artifact is active, you can take a +6 to any spell that will cause a target to Resist. You can activate the artifact by touching it and rolling Improvise, DC28. On success, up to 10 people that you can see must roll Endurance versus your spell check or be forced to Resist, as the ring draws their life force to power yours. At the GM’s discretion, lesser NPCs may simply fail automatically and be withered by the effect. When you do this, you can take a +1 bonus for each person afflicted, which applies to your next one spell per degree of success.

NAME: ARSENAL OF SECRETS
SUBJECT: CRYSTALLOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A band of colorless crystals that wrap around one arm. While this artifact is active, you can take a +4 to Read Person checks to tell if somebody is lying, and you can activate the artifact by rolling Recall Information, DC19. On success, one target must roll Concentration or Deception versus your check, and for each degree of failure on their check, you learn one piece of information they keep secret from people at large, even if it’s something you already know. For each piece of information obtained this way, one crystal on the Arsenal takes on a color, storing the secret away inside of it in a way that allows you to present it as difficult-to-forge evidence of that secret to anyone with basic crystallomancy knowledge. Although designed as the ultimate blackmail weapon, it’s most commonly used to store grocery lists. Hexologists can be forgetful. Too many hexes rattling their brains.

NAME: FALCON OF NIGHTMARE
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A small horn-like instrument crafted from a shed hoof of the legendary beast the Nightmare. You can play a somber tone in it by rolling Art, DC15, and on success, you summon a spectral falcon to alight on your shoulder and listen for the name of a person you’ve seen in the last 24 hours. Once you do, it takes off, searching for the target, and reaches them from anywhere on campus within an hour or anywhere in the world within 24 hours, at which point it streaks into them and melds with their soul. They must roll Willpower or Agility versus your check, automatically failing if targeted while asleep, or Resist Fear against your check. You can sense psychically from anywhere the status of the falcon, once it’s found its target, and whether it was successful. You cannot summon a new falcon this way until the previous one is finished.

NAME: BLOOD BLIGHT
SUBJECT: NECROMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A small cube made of the bones of a burning-eye zorvílio, a hexed creature, that floats at the ready. While this artifact is active, you learn the Wraith spell if you don’t know it already and you can take a +4 to cast it, and you can subtly activate the Blood Blight to draw a piece of somebody’s soul into the cube. Roll Stealth, DC22. On success, a target other than yourself must roll Perception or Endurance against your check or have a piece of their soul energy stolen imperceptibly into the cube. When the cube has soul energy stored in it, you can spend it without rolling to either clear a Condition stack, become invisible for 1 minute, or become capable of moving freely in three dimensions and pass through any surface that isn’t magic or antimagic for 1 minute. When you use the stored soul energy, the person whose soul it was taken from must Resist, DC20. Although a grim artifact of ancient hexological power, the cube is actually really cheerful and bouncy, and it’s super curious about its surroundings. It likes to pretend to eat too when you have a meal.

NAME: HOOK OF PROMISE
SUBJECT: RESTORATION
DESCRIPTION: A blood-red contraption worn over the back of the hand, extending with one protruding claw past the ring finger, forged of abyssal steel and fitted with a lunar platinum tip. You can drive the hook into a person’s body, including your own, and roll Improvise, DC15. An unwilling target can roll Agility versus your check to avoid the effect. For each degree of success on your check, you can force the target to Resist in exchange for giving a +4 bonus to one Ability, gaining temporary access to any Basic or Standard spell, or change their appearance. These effects last for 1 hour, and you cannot use this on the target again until the effects have worn off. If you improve someone’s Ability this way, their physical traits will often change as well to accommodate it. A classic for bioarcanist researchers who see the body as a canvas for magic effects and experimentation, but their endorsement is actually not typically very sought after.

NAME: OUBLIETTE
SUBJECT: TELEPORTATION
DESCRIPTION: A jet-black shard pendant that fills anyone other than its wielder with a cold sense of dread at the touch. While this artifact is active, you can always return instantly to the material plane without a check. You can activate the Oubliette by speaking a command phrase and rolling Comprehend, DC25. On success, a target must roll Willpower or Comprehend versus your check or be forcibly plunged into a planar subspace sealed within the pendant, which takes the form of a small room with no exits. You can enter and leave this subspace at will, and any effects that let you modify or interact with a subspace work on this one as well. Anyone trapped inside can repeat their check to escape every 24 hours, with the DC decreasing by 2 every time. You can release them at any point, but if they escape of their own volition, you must Resist against the check that broke them out.

NAME: WORD OF LAW
SUBJECT: DEMONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: An elegant golden earring, small and subtle, that gives a low golden gleam at the edges of your irises. You can activate the artifact by speaking a command to a target, rolling Willpower against the target’s, and on success, you can issue one short command and compel the target to perform it, as your eyes flare up with a brighter golden light. The command can be any action that can be completed with no check or with a single check that would cost no more than 2AP in Action. Keep in mind most people don’t like it when you do this to them! Some people are into it, though.

NAME: NIGHTMARE SPIRAL
SUBJECT: HYPNOMANCY
DESCRIPTION: A twisted, hooked spiral of extraplanar material that seems like an optical illusion to spiral infinitely smaller at one end. While this artifact is active, you cannot be put into a dreamscape without your consent, and you can activate the artifact by drawing an orb of glowing violet magic from the core and casting it at a sleeping target, where it takes the form of a spiral plunging into their mind. Roll Concentration, DC17. On a success, the target must roll Willpower or Perception versus your check or be locked into a nightmare for one hour, during which time they cannot be woken up, and forcing them to Resist a non-Wound condition at the end. When a target is locked in a nightmare this way, any Hypnomancy spells you cast on them add one degree of success. Technically a very effective insomnia cure.

NAME: BANNER OF THE TYRANT
SUBJECT: DRACONOLOGY
DESCRIPTION: A heavy, solid staff that just fits in one hand, plated with the scales of an ancient dragon. While this artifact is active, you have a +6 to all Willpower checks forced on you by a spell or other magic effect, and you can activate it to call forth a spectral banner from the center that projects your command. Roll Persuasion, DC24, and for each degree of success, one target you can see is inflicted with Command for 10 minutes or until they are no longer near you. A person under Command must roll Willpower versus your check to do anything you don’t tell them to or to ignore any command you give them, and on failure, they either obey your will or must Resist. This effect can be used on any person or on any creature with 5 or less Resolve, except for scaleworms, who are, as ever, resolutely immune to people telling them what to do.

NAME: HEART SEED
SUBJECT: ILLUSION
DESCRIPTION: A thin, delicate bracelet with a glimmering spectral energy on the edges. While this artifact is active, no effect is able to read your thoughts. You can touch the bracelet and roll Deception, DC12 to activate the artifact, forcing one target you can see to roll Comprehend against your check or have one thought implanted in their mind per degree of failure on their check. They will not necessarily give credence to the thought outright, but they will believe it to come from their own mind, although it might raise suspicion if the thought is something they would normally never think. This effect cannot be detected nor traced back to you unless they succeed with 3 or more degrees of success. You could dress the artifact up with charms to make it a cute little charm bracelet, if you want to soften the blow about casting hexes on people.

NAME: CHOKING VEIL
SUBJECT: WARDING
DESCRIPTION: A long cord that looks like thin golden fabric in a soft, delicate style, you can grasp it in both hands and draw a veil of antimagic energy from it. You can spend ten minutes without making a check to set up a small antimagic barrier that will stay as long as you leave the artifact there, or you can roll Athletics, DC14 to ensnare a target in the veil. A target must roll Agility versus your check or be caught in an antimagic snare, preventing them from casting spells or using artifacts for 1 minute per degree of failure on their check. When you ensnare someone’s magic this way, you can choose to permanently steal a piece of their magic: choose a field of magic to take a +2 bonus in while they take a -2 penalty in that field. If you take a new bonus from using the Veil again, it replaces the old one, removing the penalty from the original target.

NAME: TABULA RASA
SUBJECT: ENCHANTMENT
DESCRIPTION: A pure white pen, unadorned and, at first glance, unremarkable. While this artifact is equipped, you can place enchantments on people as on objects, and you take a +10 bonus to place enchantments this way. You can use the pen to write intricate patterns in the air, and if someone watches you do so for 10 minutes, you can roll Hide Object, DC40. On success, they must roll Recall Information versus your check or have all of their memories and personality removed and hidden away inside the Tabula Rasa, and will be blank for 10 minutes, during which time they will internalize and believe anything you tell them, acting accordingly after. You can release any person’s memories and personality that you’ve hidden away in the Tabula Rasa at any point without a check, and they will return to normal with no memories of the time they were under its effect.

NAME: BEARER OF BAD NEWS
SUBJECT: ARCANE HISTORY
DESCRIPTION: A straw figure pinned upside-down to a pentagram forged of abyssal steel small enough to fit in your hand, with a macabre smile on its face, full of tychomantic bad luck. While this artifact is active, you have more say over the outcomes of any and all Inflection Points, as long as your say over what happens is negative for the character who rolled the Inflection Point. In addition, when someone you are aware of succeeds on a roll, you can activate the artifact by driving a small nail attached to the artifact into the straw figure and roll Art versus their roll. On a success, you can turn their roll into an Inflection Point, although the roll is still considered 1 degree of success where relevant. You can always tell if someone is a hexologist, because hexologists always say the figure is “a cute lil guy.”

NAME: PLACID LAKE
SUBJECT: DIVINATION
DESCRIPTION: A small disc of lunar silver enchanted heavily with fey magics. When you can see somebody directly or via magic (but not in a recreation like a photo or a video feed), you can activate this artifact and roll Perception, DC14 to light up a waterlike surface on the disc. On success, the target must roll Comprehend versus your check, or you can learn one surface-level thought they are having per degree of failure on their check, as you skim your fingers across the surface to touch the target’s mind. Unless the target succeeds with 3 or more degrees of success, they do not notice anything. It’s not recommended to use this artifact to see what someone really thinks about your special holiday meal or if they’re just being polite, because odds are, they’re just being polite.
